jobs.title: Windows Systems Engineer - MECM / SCCM Windows Engineer (Active Top-Secret Clearance)
jobs.summary: Company Overview: Work Where it Matters Akima Mission Optimization (AMO), an Akima company, is not just another federal logistics contractor. As an Alaska Native Corporation (ANC), our mission and purpose extend beyond our exciting federal projects as we...
jobs.description:
Company Overview:

Work Where it Matters

Akima Mission Optimization (AMO), an Akima company, is not just another federal logistics contractor. As an Alaska Native Corporation (ANC), our mission and purpose extend beyond our exciting federal projects as we support our shareholder communities in Alaska.

At AMO, the work you do every day makes a difference in the lives of our 15,000 Iñupiat shareholders, a group of Alaska natives from one of the most remote and harshest environments in the United States.

For our shareholders, AMO provides support and employment opportunities and contributes to the survival of a culture that has thrived above the Arctic Circle for more than 10,000 years.

For our government customers, AMO delivers innovative administrative support services that streamline operations, and enhance productivity.

As an AMO employee, you will be surrounded by a challenging, yet supportive work environment that is committed to innovation and diversity, two of our most important values. You will also have access to our comprehensive benefits and competitive pay in addition to growth opportunities and excellent retirement options.

Description:
AMO is looking for a Windows Systems Engineer to work in North Charleston, SC. The MECM/SCCM Windows Engineer provides advanced endpoint engineering and enterprise Windows infrastructure support within the NIWC Atlantic RDT&E environment. This role is responsible for Windows image engineering, endpoint configuration management, software deployment, system integration, and sustainment across classified and unclassified networks using System Center Configuration Manager as well as Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager. To join our team of outstanding professionals, apply today!

Responsibilities:

The engineer will support multi-tenant lab environments and enterprise services while ensuring compliance with DoD cybersecurity requirements and operational continuity.
Manage and deploy Windows baseline images using SCCM/MECM.
Create and deploy task sequences in SCCM/MECM, including Windows migrations, custom image deployments, and configuration management.
Deploy third-party software using standard SCCM/MECM best practices or through third-party repositories integrated with SCCM/MECM.
Integrate enterprise tools into SCCM/MECM environments, including WSUS, BitLocker, Windows Deployment Services (WDS), ManageEngine, Dell Command, and other related tools and services.
Perform installation, configuration, maintenance, and security hardening within classified and unclassified network environments.
Conduct technical planning, system hardware/software/network design, development, integration, verification and validation (V&V), and fielding activities.
Provide technical support, administration, and monitoring of Microsoft Windows systems within physical and virtual environments.
Support operations, maintenance, and troubleshooting of end-user workstations and desktop applications.
Lead data collection, conduct analysis, and develop and implement solutions associated with incident tickets and operational requirements.
Identify and implement opportunities for continuous improvement to enhance operational effectiveness and efficiency.
Develop and document solutions to complex technical issues.
Maintain all qualification(s) required under the Cybersecurity Workforce (CSWF) framework for Privileged Access Administrator (PAA) roles.
Qualifications:

Active Top-Secret Clearance.
7+ years of experience supporting enterprise infrastructure in a DoD environment.
Experience administering and engineering Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM/SCCM).
Experience supporting Windows enterprise environments in classified and unclassified networks.
Ability to perform technical planning, system integration, and validation activities.
DoD 8570/8140 compliant certification (e.g., Security+)

Desired Certifications / Training:

Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (MECM) Training (23 hours).
Windows Server 2019 or 2022 administration training.
Windows 11 administration training.
Job ID: 2026-23367 Work Type: On-Site

jobs.description:
Position Description

We are seeking a skilled Windows Endpoint Engineer with deep expertise in managing Windows environments using Workspace ONE. This role involves providing Tier 2/3 support for Windows 10/11 systems, both physical and virtual, and playing a key role in deploying and managing MDM solutions. The ideal candidate will have hands on experience with Workspace ONE console operations and a strong understanding of endpoint security, compliance, and automation.

We partner with 15 of the top 20 banks globally, and our top 10 banking clients have worked with us for an average of 26 years!.

This role is located at a client site in Plano, TX. A hybrid working model is acceptable.

Your future duties and responsibilities

We are seeking a skilled Windows Endpoint Engineer with deep expertise in managing Windows environments using Workspace ONE. This role involves providing Tier 2/3 support for Windows 10/11 systems, both physical and virtual, and playing a key role in deploying and managing MDM solutions. The ideal candidate will have hands on experience with Workspace ONE console operations and a strong understanding of endpoint security, compliance, and automation.

Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

2+ years of experience in Windows desktop engineering or support Strong troubleshooting skills for Windows 10/11 environments Hands on experience with Workspace ONE, including console operations (creating profiles, sensors, smart groups, baselines) Familiarity with endpoint management protocols: CSP, SyncML, WNS, OMA DM Experience with Windows OS in place upgrades and Windows Update for Business Proficiency in Group Policy and CSP based configuration management Understanding of Windows Autopilot, Azure AD Join, and hybrid environments Basic scripting skills (preferably PowerShell) for automation and reporting Solid grasp of ITIL Foundation principles, including service lifecycle and continual improvement Strong analytical thinking and problem solving capabilities Excellent communication and documentation skills ITIL Foundation certification preferred

Education:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field.

jobs.title: Senior Systems Engineer (Windows Endpoint Mangement)
jobs.summary: About Gartner IT: Join a world-class team of skilled engineers who build creative digital solutions to support our colleagues and clients. We make a broad organizational impact by delivering cutting-edge technology solutions that power Gartner. Gartner IT...
jobs.description:
About Gartner IT:

Join a world-class team of skilled engineers who build creative digital solutions to support our colleagues and clients. We make a broad organizational impact by delivering cutting-edge technology solutions that power Gartner. Gartner IT values its culture of nonstop innovation, an outcome-driven approach to success, and the notion that great ideas can come from anyone on the team.

About the role:

The Windows Endpoint team is looking for an engineer to help manage the Windows fleet including endpoint management, patching, administration and automation using technologies such as Intune, Configuration Manager, and PowerShell.

What you will do

Design, implement and maintain our Windows-based endpoint environment, including endpoint management, patch management, security management and deployment automation

Evaluate new technologies related to end user computing for use in the corporate enterprise to enable our knowledge workers

Develop standard work environments on company owned devices to ensure usability, performance and security requirements are maintained

Automate end user support activities to include packaging of software and scripting of support tasks

Automate service desk and desktop support tasks using various scripting languages (PowerShell)

Assist with packaging all Gartner owned software for deployment via Enterprise distribution methods using Configuration Manager (formerly System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM)) and Microsoft Intune

Leverage DEX (Digital Employee Experience) tools to proactively optimize and manage the employee experience on Windows endpoints.

Lead the design and development of DEX-based remote actions and dashboards to drive actionable insights and improve operational efficiency.

Administration of Configuration Manager and Intune to include metrics/reporting using various tools

Leverage AI-driven tools (e.g., Gemini, Copilot) to assist in scripting, diagnostics, hypothesis generation, and resolution drafting.

Stay up-to-date with industry trends and emerging endpoint security and management technologies

Work with the team to coach Gartner's internal support teams via email communications, videos, virtual training sessions, and the creation/maintenance of knowledgebase articles and support documentation.

Work within ITIL Change Management process to shepherd planned changes

What you will need :

2+ years managing the Automation of tasks using various scripting languages (PowerShell) to better support the service desk and desktop support

3+ years of experience using Intune/Configuration Manager to manage and deploy Windows devices

Packaging and distribution of applications using Configuration Manager.

Experience with Windows Patching using Windows Autopatch/Windows Update for Business, WSUS/CM, Software Update Groups (SUG), and Automatic Deployment Rules (ADR), 3rd party patching.

Good to have:

Hybrid Active Directory, Azure Active Directory

Microsoft Azure

2+ years of experience managing devices using Configuration Manager (aka CM, SCCM, MECM)

Experience using Autopilot to deploy Windows devices via Microsoft Intune

Supporting Windows 365 Cloud PCs, Azure Virtual Desktop (Windows Virtual Desktop) or another similar VDI solution (Citrix, Amazon Workspaces)

Active Directory Users and Computers

PowerBI and reporting

Intune Configuration policies/Group Policy

Experience with DEX (Digital Employee Experience) tools

Experience with Microsoft Windows

Experience with Microsoft Office 365 and Office Configuration Manager

Who you are:

Graduate/Post graduate in BE/Btech, ME/MTech or MCA is preferred.

Excellent communication and prioritization skills.

Able to work independently or within a team proactively in a fast-paced AGILE-SCRUM environment.

Owns success – Takes responsibility for successful delivery of the solutions.

Strong desire to improve upon their skills in software development, frameworks, and technologies

What you will get:

Competitive compensation.

Limitless growth and learning opportunities.

Ongoing mentorship and apprenticeship; Leadership courses, development programs, technical courses, certification opportunities and more!

A collaborative and positive culture - join a diverse team of professionals that are as smart and driven as you.

A chance to make an impact – your work will contribute directly to our strategy.

Hybrid Work Environment - enjoy the flexibility of working from home and the energy of collaborating with peers in our dynamic offices.

20+ PTO days plus holidays and floating holidays in your first year.

Extensive medical, dental insurance and vision plan.

401K with corporate match, immediate vesting.

Health-and-wellness-related allowance programs.

Parental leave.

Tuition reimbursement.

Employee Stock Purchase Plan.

Employee Assistance Program.

Gartner Gives Charity Match.
And much more!

#LI-Hybrid
#LI-TW1

Who are we?

At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.

Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.

Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 20,000 associates globally who support over 13,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.

What makes Gartner a great place to work?

Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.

We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.

Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.

We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.

Gartner is the world authority on AI

At Gartner, you’ll join a company at the very center of the AI revolution. Gartner has proactive, objective guidance throughout clients’ AI journeys. We set the standard for how organizations leverage artificial intelligence to drive meaningful impact. You’ll have access to unmatched resources, expertise, and technology, and play a key role in helping Gartner and our clients innovate and grow as we leverage AI to transform business and technology landscapes.

It’s an exciting time to be at Gartner, with limitless opportunities to make a real impact, grow your skills, and build a lasting, meaningful career in a field that’s reshaping the way we operate. If you’re passionate about AI and want to be part of a team that’s guiding the leaders who shape the world, Gartner is the place for you.

What do we offer?

Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.

In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.

Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.

Gartner believes in fair and equitable pay. A reasonable estimate of the base salary range for this role is 86,000 USD - 118,000 USD. Please note that actual salaries may vary within the range, or be above or below the range, based on factors including, but not limited to, education, training, experience, professional achievement, business need, and location. In addition to base salary, employees will participate in either an annual bonus plan based on company and individual performance, or a role-based, uncapped sales incentive plan. Our talent acquisition team will provide the specific opportunity on our bonus or incentive programs to eligible candidates. jobs.title: Client Platform Engineering Lead | Digital Asset Trading Innovator
jobs.summary: [Up to c. £300k Comp Package | Hybrid Working] Role Overview We’re working with a high-growth trading and digital-assets firm seeking a seasoned engineering leader to take ownership of their client-facing technology ecosystem. As the organisation expands...
jobs.description:
[Up to c. £300k Comp Package | Hybrid Working]

Role Overview

We’re working with a high-growth trading and digital-assets firm seeking a seasoned engineering leader to take ownership of their client-facing technology ecosystem. As the organisation expands into new markets and contends with evolving regulatory demands, this role will spearhead the architecture and delivery of platforms that underpin client onboarding, reporting, data exchange, and compliance workflows. You’ll guide the engineering strategy for a suite of internal and external systems - including CRM tooling, realtime reporting pipelines, API integrations, client portals, and surveillance utilities - while coaching a small but high-impact engineering team. This is a hands-on leadership position where you’ll set the roadmap, refine standards, and shape how the firm delivers secure, reliable, and regulation-ready client technologies...

Key Responsibilities

Define and execute the engineering strategy for client-facing systems spanning CRM workflows, reporting platforms, and external integration points Lead, mentor, and develop a team of software engineers, fostering strong delivery discipline, resilience, and technical growth Partner closely with commercial and operational stakeholders to understand client needs and translate them into scalable, maintainable solutions Oversee the design and build of distributed services and APIs that support both realtime and batch data exchange with counterparties Own the engineering roadmap for the client web portal, ensuring robust authentication, data integrity, auditability, and consistent user experience Manage the lifecycle of compliance-aligned platforms such as trade-surveillance modules, transaction-monitoring systems, document repositories, and ID-verification capabilities Drive best practices across code quality, architecture, observability, and secure software development Ensure systems meet regulatory requirements relating to data handling, retention, reporting, and operational resilience Coordinate delivery across multiple concurrent projects, maintaining clear communication with technical and non-technical stakeholders Evaluate third-party technologies, influence vendor selection, and oversee integration efforts in partnership with legal and compliance teams What You’ll Bring…

7-12 years of engineering experience, including time as an Engineering Manager, Technical Lead, or Senior Engineer in financial services, trading, fintech, or another high-complexity environment Demonstrated ability to architect and scale distributed systems, client-serving APIs, or multi-component full-stack platforms Strong technical judgment and the ability to translate ambiguous requirements into clear design decisions Hands-on experience working with web technologies (e.g. modern JavaScript frameworks), backend services (Python, Go, or JVM languages), data-integration pipelines, and cloud-native tooling Understanding of reconciliation workflows, data lineage, and the operational safeguards required for systems that must be accurate, auditable, and failure-tolerant Proven ability to work effectively in regulated settings, with familiarity around data-protection, security controls, and governance practices typical in finance/fintech Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate with senior stakeholders across business, compliance, and engineering A practical, product-focused mindset - solving business problems with technology, not the other way around ...

Experience: 5 years required

jobs.title: Associate UX Designer – Client Platform
jobs.summary: Associate UX Designer – Client Platform Wealth Management Platforms Purchase, New York We are seeking an Associate UX Designer to join our Wealth Management Client UX team that supports self-directed and FA managed clients. In this role, you will help...
jobs.description: Associate UX Designer – Client Platform Wealth Management Platforms Purchase, New York We are seeking an Associate UX Designer to join our Wealth Management Client UX team that supports self-directed and FA managed clients. In this role, you will help design data-rich experiences that give clients a clear picture of their portfolios and account data. You will also design analytics tools to give users a deeper understanding of their finances. You’ll contribute across the end-to-end product design lifecycle, partnering closely with UX leads, researchers, product owners, and engineers. You will be focused on translating business requirements into intuitive interfaces using components from our design system. This is a hands-on role responsible for user journeys, screen flows, wireframes, prototypes and high-fidelity designs. This role is required to work in-office a minimum of 4 days a week to support teamwork and collaboration. About Morgan Stanley Morgan Stanley is a leading global financial services firm providing a wide range of investment banking, securities, investment management and wealth management services. The Firm’s employees serve clients worldwide including corporations, governments and individuals from more than 1,200 offices in 43 countries. Morgan Stanley is committed to helping its employees build meaningful careers and we strive to be a place for people to learn, achieve and grow. Department Overview In the Wealth Management division, we help people, businesses and institutions build, preserve, and manage wealth so they can pursue their financial goals Wealth Management (WM) Platforms manages industry-leading platforms, across all WM channels and client segments, to provide a unified digital experience, unlock growth, and deliver efficiencies for Advisors, Clients, and Institutions. WM Platforms consists of ten sub-teams including: Field Experience & Platforms, Digital Client Experience & Platforms, Workplace Platforms, Automation & Workflow, Digital Trading & Investing, Generative AI, UX Design & Research, Strategy & Execution, WM Platforms Risk, and the Chief Operating Office. Morgan Stanley Wealth Management’s User Experience (UX) team designs digital experiences that help clients understand their finances, monitor investments, and act with confidence across Morgan Stanley and E*TRADE from Morgan Stanley platforms. We design for two primary audiences: clients and Financial Advisors, who guide clients through important financial milestones We are a growing team of designers and researchers who bring an entrepreneurial mindset to a leading global financial firm. We use design thinking and a user-centered approach to solve real customer and business problems—often in complex, highly regulated environments. If you enjoy bringing clarity to complexity, focusing on the details, and building experiences that fit seamlessly into a large product ecosystem, we’d like to meet you. What you’ll do in the role: Partner with the UX lead and cross-functional teams to deliver experiences that balance user needs, business objectives, and platform constraints Translate complex business rules, edge cases, and user scenarios into simple screen flows and intuitive interface designs Create and iterate on design artifacts including journey maps, task flows, wireframes, high-fidelity mockups, and interactive prototypes Design within an established ecosystem by leveraging existing interaction patterns and design system components to maintain consistency across products Work closely with our research team to identify user pain-points and ideate ways to improve the product experience Deliver responsive/adaptive designs optimized for web and mobile, and collaborate with engineering to support accurate implementation (including specs and design QA) Demonstrate strong attention to detail—creating a strong visual hierarchy with typography and layout, as well as considering error handling and empty states Advocate for inclusive design by aligning work to accessibility expectations (e.g., WCAG/ADA) and supporting usability for a wide range of user abilities Use company-approved AI tools to concept, iterate and quickly prototype workflows Ask thoughtful questions, seek feedback, and continuously grow design craft and product understanding Articulate the rationale behind your design decisions Champion design system thinking across multiple tabs and products over isolated page level interactions Collaborate with UX designers on other teams to share ideas, provide feedback, align on common patterns Skills Required: You have an outstanding portfolio that demonstrates how you have created experiences that solved business and user needs You’ve spent 2-5 years as a UX designer who was a key contributor to the success of products that have launched You have a bachelor's degree in design or related field preferably, master’s degree a plus You have excellent skills in Figma You thrive in a collaborative, explorative environment and you value being on a diverse team to brainstorm and get feedback from within a large organization You practice active listening and contribute in-group situations, like brainstorms, audits, critiques, and whiteboard sessions You get excited by complex projects and can navigate a path forward when there is ambiguity You ask questions to clarify your understanding of the business rules, use cases and end users WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM MORGAN STANLEY: At Morgan Stanley, we raise, manage and allocate capital for our clients – helping them reach their goals. We do it in a way that’s differentiated – and we’ve done that for 90 years. Our values - putting clients first, doing the right thing, leading with exceptional ideas, committing to diversity and inclusion, and giving back - aren’t just beliefs, they guide the decisions we make every day to do what's best for our clients, communities and more than 80,000 employees in 1,200 offices across 42 countries. At Morgan Stanley, you’ll find an opportunity to work alongside the best and the brightest, in an environment where you are supported and empowered. Our teams are relentless collaborators and creative thinkers, fueled by their diverse backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to support our employees and their families at every point along their work-life journey, offering some of the most attractive and comprehensive employee benefits and perks in the industry. There’s also ample opportunity to move about the business for those who show passion and grit in their work. To learn more about our offices across the globe, please copy and paste https://www.morganstanley.com/about-us/global-offices into your browser. Expected base pay rates for the role will be between$58,000 and $115,000 per year at the commencement of employment. However, base pay if hired will be determined on an individualized basis and is only part of the total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may also include commission earnings, incentive compensation, discretionary bonuses, other short and long-term incentive packages, and other Morgan Stanley sponsored benefit programs. Morgan Stanley is an equal opportunity employer committed to building and maintaining a workforce that is diverse in experience and background. Our recruiting efforts reflect our strong commitment to a culture of inclusion, where individuals are hired, developed, and advanced based on their skills and talents. Our workforce reflects a broad cross-section of the global communities in which we operate, bringing a variety of backgrounds, talents, perspectives, and experiences. For more information, please visit: https://www.morganstanley.com/people-opportunities/eeo. At Morgan Stanley, we advise, originate, trade, manage and distribute capital for people, governments and institutions, always with a standard of excellence and guided by our core values. Morgan Stanley is dedicated to providing first-class service to our clients, in a way that reflects our commitment to creating a more sustainable future and fostering stronger communities around the world. In each line of business, we strive to demonstrate our belief in the power of transformative thinking, innovative strategies and leading-edge solutions—and in the ability of capital to work for the benefit of all society. What We Do

jobs.description:
Summary

Our client is seeking an Infrastructure Engineer to design, implement, and support the organization's enterprise infrastructure including virtual environments, servers, and core systems. This role will ensure the stability, security, and performance of the IT environment while leading infrastructure projects, supporting disaster recovery initiatives, and providing technical expertise across the organization.

Responsibilities

Design, implement, administer, and optimize enterprise infrastructure including virtual servers, operating systems, and Core applications.Manage VMware environments, virtual machines, host hardware, Microsoft server platforms, and enterprise software.Monitor system performance, troubleshoot complex infrastructure issues, and implement performance improvements.Develop and maintain disaster recovery and business continuity solutions including testing and documentation.Create and maintain technical documentation, deployment procedures, and infrastructure standards.Support system upgrades, patching, migrations, and infrastructure enhancement projects while following change management processes.Perform advanced troubleshooting for servers, desktop systems, and enterprise applications.Research, evaluate, and implement new technologies through proof-of-concept testing and technical recommendations.Partner with business and IT teams throughout the project lifecycle to design reliable technical solutions.Mentor junior team members and provide technical guidance on infrastructure best practices.

Requirements

7+ years of experience in enterprise systems administration or infrastructure engineering.Extensive experience with VMware vSphere, vCenter, virtual machine administration, and virtualization technologies.Experience administering Microsoft Windows server environments and enterprise infrastructure.Knowledge of SQL Server administration, Microsoft Exchange, Citrix, and enterprise monitoring tools.Solid understanding of networking, security, disaster recovery, and business continuity best practices.Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and communication skills with the ability to work collaboratively across technical teams.

Preferred

Experience with infrastructure automation, scripting, and system implementation projects.Industry certifications including VCP, MCSE, CCA-V, CASP, or similar.

This is a Direct Hire opportunity with our Topeka, KS client. Employee benefits include Medical/Dental Benefits, Paid time off, Paid Holidays, and 401(k). H1-B Visa sponsorship is not available for this position. No third-parties, please.

jobs.title: Principal Infrastructure Engineer
jobs.summary: Consequential Work. Dedicated People. About Onebrief Onebrief builds collaboration and AI-powered workflow software for military planning and operational coordination. Today, many critical planning workflows still rely on fragmented systems, static...
jobs.description:
Consequential Work. Dedicated People.
About Onebrief
Onebrief builds collaboration and AI-powered workflow software for military planning and operational coordination.

Today, many critical planning workflows still rely on fragmented systems, static documents, and disconnected tools that make collaboration and decision-making unnecessarily difficult. Onebrief brings modern software, AI, and real-time collaboration into those environments, helping teams operate with greater clarity, coordination, and adaptability in situations where decisions carry real-world consequences.

We are a distributed team of builders from military, operational, and technology backgrounds who care deeply about improving how important work gets done. Some team members work remotely, while others work directly alongside customers in operational environments around the world.

Founded in 2019, Onebrief is backed by leading investors including General Catalyst, Battery Ventures, Insight Partners, Sapphire Ventures, and Human Capital. Valued at more than $2 billion, we continue to invest in product innovation, AI capabilities, and team growth.

About the RoleOnebrief's infrastructure team owns the platform that delivers our software to military staffs. Our charter spans Kubernetes clusters in commercial and government cloud, application streaming for thin-client users on classified networks, a unified artifact pipeline that signs and ships the same builds to every environment we operate, and air-gapped appliances at forward sites.

We're hiring an Infrastructure Engineer who builds security into the systems they operate rather than treating it as a separate workstream. You'll architect, build, and run the platform end to end. This includes RFCs, PRDs, and reviewing and writing production code alongside Product and Engineering teammates. The work cuts across cloud-native deployments, edge appliances, data platforms, and the application streaming layer that puts Onebrief on classified environments. You'll partner with Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) and mission owners on STIGs, CVE remediation, audit prep, and the controls work that lets us operate in environments such as IL5, IL6, and JWICS.

Success means a platform that stays up under real operational load, ships trusted artifacts to every profile we run, and passes audits without fire drills.

About YouPeople by-name request you because you bias towards action. You are a problem-solver by nature who genuinely cares about the mission and the outcome. You operate without hand-holding. You read a problem, weigh tradeoffs, and move. Sometimes the tradeoffs have harsh consequences that you pay for later, but you are resilient and adapt. When the work crosses into expertise you don't have, you dig into the problem, pull in the right teammates, apply their feedback, and continue.

What You'll DoDesign, develop, test, iterate, and deploy secure production systems across cloud-native and edge appliance deployments

Embed with cross-functional teams and advise on infrastructure, security, and deployment best practices that hold up in production

Own end-to-end infrastructure outcomes for one or more critical programs or priority infrastructure initiatives

Harden the artifact pipeline so the same signed builds run in cloud, single-tenant high-side, and air-gapped appliance deployments

What We Look ForYou'll do well in this role if you bring:

8+ years operating production infrastructure in DevOps/DevSecOps, Platform, SRE, Cloud, or Infrastructure role

Full-stack engineering experience when necessary, e.g., production experience designing, building, and operating services in a modern backend language (e.g., Go, Python, Rust, TypeScript)

Deep production Kubernetes experience: operators, networking, storage, multi-cluster operations, and a feel for what breaks at scale

Expertise with major cloud providers (e.g., Amazon AWS, Microsoft Azure), with strong instincts for designing across providers and profiles

Strong application and infrastructure security fundamentals: identity, network segmentation, secrets management, common vulnerability classes, and sound security judgment under ambiguity

Demonstrated ability to build working solutions from scratch, connect disparate applications together, and jump into existing code bases to add value

Experience packaging and shipping workloads into disconnected or air-gapped environments

Demonstrated leadership/mentorship experience

Ability to obtain a SECRET clearance

Nice to have qualifications:

Active US SECRET or TOP SECRET security clearance

Government cloud expertise (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government)

FedRAMP, IL4/IL5, IL6, or JWICS environment exposure

Audit experience (FedRAMP, SOC 2, or equivalent)

Big data and distributed data experience

Prior experience supporting defense, intelligence, or other regulated industries

Notice to Third Party Recruitment Agencies
Please note that Onebrief does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the absence of an executed Recruitment Services Agreement, there will be no obligation to any referral compensation or recruiter fee. In the event a recruiter or agency submits a resume or candidate without an agreement Onebrief explicitly reserves the right to pursue and hire those candidate(s) without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. Any unsolicited resumes, including those submitted to hiring managers, shall be deemed the property of Onebrief.

jobs.description:
Job Description

The Technology Architecture & Engineering Department is responsible for worldwide technical strategy, design, implementation, and top level support for all core infrastructure. As part of TA&E, the End-User Computing Engineering group is responsible for maintaining all End-User platforms: Windows desktop, virtualized desktops (server and desktop hosted) and mobile devices.

Primary Responsibilities and/or Essential functions:

Lead the management and integration of firm productivity software (e.g., iManage Work, Litera Compare, MS Office products, Office add-ins, etc.)Work closely with internal IT teams and product vendors to design and setup applications to meet firm security and performance standardsDetermine appropriate requirements for application configurations and validate through end-to-end testingDiagnose, troubleshoot and resolve issues across a wide array of hardware and software productsBe the top level responsible party for escalated support, working directly with external vendors and consultants to resolve issuesEfficiently triage and prioritize tasks, projects and issues in a highly demanding environmentAny additional responsibilities as required by management.

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:

Advanced knowledge of Windows operating system internals and Microsoft based applications and technologiesAdvanced knowledge of using and integrating productivity suite applicationsExperience working with registry, file system, and configuration changes required for enterprise application deploymentsAdvanced troubleshooting experience across Windows applications, including performance, compatibility, and integration issuesExperience supporting 2nd/3rd level escalations, coordinating with vendors to drive root-cause resolutionExperience managing Windows devices using Microsoft Intune / Endpoint ManagerExperience building and troubleshooting Win32 app packages (.intunewin)Experience with application performance monitoring (APM) and SIEM solutions such as Aternity and SplunkUnderstanding of identity, security, and access controls as they relate to end-user platforms (Azure AD, Conditional Access, etc.)Understanding of network protocols, including TCP/IP, DHCP, DNS, HTTP(S), SMB, and other application-layer protocolsUnderstanding of API usage between applicationsExperience with scripting (e.g., PowerShell, VB) a plusExperience supporting MacOS a plus5+ years of experience in information technology, including 2+ years of experience in engineeringStrong problem-solving and analytical skillsProven experience with large enterprise scale environmentsProven desire and ability to learn new technologiesProven success in leading projectsProven focus on customer service and business oriented thinking

Education/Certifications:

Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, physics, or a similar field

Estimated salary range is $150,000 - $200,000. Compensation decisions within the range are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case including but not limited to the individual's skills, experience, and qualifications.

Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP offers a competitive compensation package comprised of base pay and discretionary year-end bonus for eligible employees. Benefits include, but are not limited to medical, dental, vision, disability coverage, life insurance, flexible spending plan and a 401K plan. Weil also offers generous paid time off and holidays. All decisions affecting employment at Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP are made on the basis of qualification, performance and other pertinent work-related factors, and without discrimination against any person on the basis of race, color, sex, age, religion, national origin, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, veteran’s status, genetic information or any other legally protected status.

jobs.title: Senior Systems Engineer, End-User Computing
jobs.summary: At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience. Delta is focused on sustaining a...
jobs.description:
At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.
Delta is focused on sustaining a strong IT operation, growing our capabilities, and maximizing optimization across each of our tech hubs to elevate the travel experience for our customers and empower our 90,000 Delta people. We’re committed to fostering innovation, and we’re excited to invite you to be part of our journey as we shape the future of technology at the world’s best airline!
A Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is an experienced technical specialist responsible for the architecture, deployment, support, and continuous improvement of secure, efficient end-user computing environments across an organization. This role covers a broad spectrum of EUC technologies, including desktop virtualization, device management, security, application delivery, and end-user support, ensuring optimal user experience and productivity. Senior EUC Engineers often serve as technical leads, mentoring junior staff and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver seamless digital workspaces.
Key Responsibilities EUC Architecture and Design: Develop and maintain strategic EUC infrastructure designs, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with business needs. Evaluate, recommend, and implement new technologies and solutions. Technical Implementation and Support: Lead the deployment, configuration, and maintenance of EUC solutions such as VDI (Virtual Desktop Infrastructure), application packaging, and endpoint security platforms. Incident Management and Troubleshooting: Serve as an escalation point for complex technical issues, diagnosing and resolving incidents related to desktops, laptops, virtual machines, and application delivery. Automation and Scripting: Develop and maintain automation scripts and tools to streamline EUC operations, increase efficiency, and reduce manual intervention. Security and Compliance: Implement and enforce policies for device and data security, ensuring compliance with organizational standards and regulatory requirements. Participate in security audits and remediation. Collaboration and Leadership: Work closely with IT leadership, service desk, infrastructure, and security teams. Mentor junior engineers and contribute to technical knowledge sharing and documentation. Performance Optimization: Monitor EUC environments for performance, capacity, and availability, proactively identifying improvements or upgrades to maximize user satisfaction. Project Management: Lead or participate in EUC-related projects, from requirements gathering through design, implementation, and post-implementation support. Provide status updates and documentation throughout the project lifecycle. End-User Experience: Partner with business units to understand user requirements and deliver solutions that enhance productivity and usability, ensuring minimal disruption during change or migration activities. Documentation and Reporting: Maintain comprehensive technical documentation, configurations, and procedures for EUC infrastructure and services. Generate regular reports on system health, incidents, and improvement initiatives.

jobs.title: Senior End-User Computing Engineer
jobs.summary: Overview The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for overseeing the organization's end-user computing environment, ensuring the efficient operation, security, and optimization of desktop, laptop, and virtual desktop infrastructure...
jobs.description:
Overview

The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for overseeing the organization's end-user computing environment, ensuring the efficient operation, security, and optimization of desktop, laptop, and virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI). This role involves advanced troubleshooting, leadership in project management, and collaborating with IT teams on technology strategy and integration. The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer will act as a key technical expert in designing and implementing complex desktop solutions across the enterprise. Pay range for the role is $140,000 to $150,000, with an annual bonus. The role can be 100% remote anywhere in United States.

Responsibilities

Advanced Desktop Infrastructure Management:Architect, deploy and maintain the organization's desktop environments, including Windows-based physical and virtual desktops (VDI).Develop and manage automated desktop deployments using tools such as Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) or equivalent technologies.Design, implement, and maintain desktop images, ensuring they are optimized for performance and security.Leadership and Strategy:Lead desktop infrastructure projects, such as OS migrations, VDI implementations, and major software rollouts.Provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior engineers and support staff.Collaborate with senior IT leadership to develop and implement long-term desktop technology strategies.Security and Compliance:Ensure compliance with internal security policies, industry standards, and audit requirements.Lead initiatives to enhance desktop security, including endpoint protection, encryption, and vulnerability management.Manage group policies, endpoint security software, and patch management processes using WSUS or other tools.Troubleshooting and Escalation:Serve as the highest escalation point for complex desktop-related issues, providing Level 3 support.Perform in-depth root cause analysis for recurring or complex technical problems and implement preventative measures.Provide expert support for remote access solutions, VPNs, and cloud desktop services.Automation and Efficiency:Develop and implement automation scripts using PowerShell and other tools to streamline desktop management tasks.Continuously seek opportunities to improve end-user computing efficiency, including desktop performance tuning and reducing downtime.System Integration:Collaborate with server, network, and security teams to ensure the seamless integration of desktop systems into the broader IT infrastructure.Ensure optimal configuration and performance of desktop environments within cloud services like Microsoft 365 and Azure Active Directory.Manage and integrate virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware Horizon, Citrix, Hyper-V).Documentation and Knowledge Sharing:Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for desktop systems, policies, and procedures.Ensure knowledge sharing and training for IT staff on new technologies, best practices, and troubleshooting techniques.Actively participate in developing and updating standard operating procedures (SOPs) for desktop support.
Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent experience).5+ years of experience in desktop engineering or support, with significant experience in enterprise-level desktop management.Demonstrated experience leading major desktop infrastructure projects, including large-scale deployments, migrations, and VDI implementation.Expertise in Microsoft technologies, including SCCM, Intune, and Active Directory.Advanced knowledge of Windows 10/11, Group Policy, and desktop management tools.Strong PowerShell scripting skills for automation and troubleshooting.Experience with virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware, Citrix, Hyper-V) and virtual desktop environments (VDI).Familiarity with cloud-based services and integration (Microsoft 365, Azure AD, etc.).Strong leadership and team collaboration skills.Ability to manage multiple priorities and projects in a fast-paced environment.Excellent problem-solving and critical thinking abilities.Outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal.Certifications, Licenses, and/or RegistrationMicrosoft Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator ExpertMicrosoft Certified: Azure Fundamentals or Administrator AssociateVMware VCP or Citrix certifications.ITIL Certification or other relevant service management certifications.Physical Demands and Work EnvironmentThe physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit and use hands to handle, touch or feel objects, tools, or controls. The employee frequently is required to talk and hear. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate. The employee is occasionally required to stand; walk; reach with hands and arms. The employee is rarely required to stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, and the ability to adjust focus.

jobs.title: Digital Workplace Service Engineer
jobs.summary: Job Description: Overview: We don’t simply hire employees. We invest in them. When you work at Chatham, we empower you — offering professional development opportunities to help you grow in your career, no matter if you've been here for five months or 15...
jobs.description: Job Description: Overview: We don’t simply hire employees. We invest in them. When you work at Chatham, we empower you — offering professional development opportunities to help you grow in your career, no matter if you've been here for five months or 15 years. Chatham has worked hard to create a distinct work environment that values people, teamwork, integrity, and client service. You will have immediate opportunities to partner with talented subject matter experts, work on complex projects, and contribute to the value Chatham delivers every day. The Digital Workplace Engineer is part of the Digital Workplace Services team within Chatham’s IT organization, focused on creating a modern, seamless technology experience for employees across the firm. The team supports all internal users, empowering better communication and collaboration through tools like Microsoft 365, Teams, Sharepoint, and AI-driven solutions. In this individual contributor role, you’ll act as both a technology champion and trusted advisor—driving adoption of new tools, sharing best practices, and helping employees unlock the full value of the firm’s workplace technologies. In this role you will: Enthusiastically promote advanced technology solutions to employees, ensuring they understand and appreciate the benefits and usefulness of our end-user tech through various media, including articles, webinars, videos, in-person events, and more. Actively engage with vendors and the tech community to leverage best practices, learn industry trends, and stay ahead of technological advancements. Provide insightful user feedback to IT leadership to drive strategic product and engineering decisions. Partner with IT to lead the development of standards and best practices, serving as the authoritative voice of our end users. Manage end-user satisfaction and the adoption of M365, Teams, conference room equipment, digital signage, IPTV services, and telephony services. Lead governance, standards, cost models/chargeback, and citizen development within the Microsoft 365 suite. Take responsibility for key performance indicators to drive end-user satisfaction and engagement with technology. Lead engagements with the business to understand challenges and propose solutions for process improvement and business process automation. Your impact: Serve as a technical leader, advocating for users to deliver products and services that meet their needs Lead innovation within the company by providing the appropriate tools and training to enable better communication and collaboration Help drive business process automation through consulting and by delivering tools and guidelines for self-service Be a key contributor to developing IT’s vision and roadmap for workplace productivity technology Contributors to your success: More than 5 years of experience in a similar role with a proven track record of successfully leading technology adoption and major technology deployments. Exceptional communication skills with the ability to gather requirements from and provide IT training to non-technical employees. Deep expertise in the Microsoft Office 365 suite of products. Demonstrated ability to solve complex problems through automation, preferably with Microsoft’s Power Platform. Strong emphasis on customer service and the ability to teach people of all technical abilities. Experience working as a technical lead on major technology deployment and adoption efforts. Experience with Lean process improvement is a plus For Denver based candidates, the compensation range for the position is expected to be between $82,000 and $100,000 annually. Total compensation, including base pay, discretionary individual bonus and company bonus, may be higher than range listed, depending on applicant’s skills, qualifications, and experience. Benefits include health insurance, life and disability insurance, 401k, EAP, paid holidays and paid time off. About Chatham Financial: Chatham Financial is the leading independent capital markets advisor, delivering an integrated blend of expert advice and powerful technology to help you reduce risk and seize opportunity. With decades of capital markets strategy, execution, monitoring, and performance expertise, we serve as an unwavering advocate for your best interests and your innovation partner. Our technology platform unifies data across assets, debt, and derivatives, giving you unmatched agility, transparency, and insight. It’s clear ahead. Our commitment is to carry that light forward in every partnership, every solution, and every market we serve. We help guide the way—giving clients the insight and momentum to move forward with confidence, no matter what lies ahead. jobs.title: Digital Workplace Engineering Lead
jobs.summary: About the position As a Digital Workplace Engineering Lead you will be a hands‑on technical leader responsible for the operational excellence, design, implementation and optimization of enterprise digital workplace solutions. You’ll apply deep technical...
jobs.description:
About the position

As a Digital Workplace Engineering Lead you will be a hands‑on technical leader responsible for the operational excellence, design, implementation and optimization of enterprise digital workplace solutions. You’ll apply deep technical expertise across one or more workplace domains while providing technical leadership, mentoring, and strategic influence across teams and stakeholders.

Responsibilities

Drive operational excellence You will lead complex incident response and post‑mortems; implement corrective actions to prevent recurrence. You will champion automation, monitoring, and standardized engineering practices. You will partner cross‑functionally to improve reliability, cost effectiveness, and speed to delivery.Design and engineer secure, reliable services You will design, implement, and optimize scalable, reliable, secure, compliant workplace services. You will review and influence technical designs to meet best practices, performance standards, and security requirements. You will use service health metrics to remove bottlenecks and improve resilience and scalability.Lead technology strategy and roadmaps You will serve as a subject matter expert and provide design guidance across initiatives. You will drive technology strategy including lifecycle management, roadmap planning, adoption, and continuous improvement. You will research industry trends and emerging technologies to shape future-state capabilities.Collaborate and influence You will partner with business leaders, IT, security, compliance, and office services to deliver integrated solutions. You will communicate technical risks, tradeoffs, and recommendations clearly to senior leadership. You will influence standardized technologies and enterprise engineering practices across teams.Mentor and uplift the team You will provide coaching and mentorship; foster a culture of knowledge sharing. You will support hiring, onboarding, and development of new associates. You will model innovation, collaboration, continuous improvement, and customer focus.Requirements

Real‑Time Communications & Audio Visual experience: conferencing platforms (Teams/Zoom/Webex), VC conferencing room systems (Cisco, MTR), desk audio and webcam devicesWorkplace Engagement Technology experience: digital signage and experience platforms, office/space toolsGenAI Enablement experience: secure, compliant AI‑powered content creation platformsCompliance & Data Analytics experience: electronic communications compliance and regulatory solutions for real-time media.advanced technical expertise in at least one digital workplace engineering domainexperience delivering large‑scale, enterprise technology solutionsstrategic planning and analytical problem solvingunderstanding of security, compliance, and IT service management practices (e.g., ITIL)strong written/verbal communication with senior leader engagementexperience mentoring engineers and influencing across teamsexperience leading cross‑department transformationsuser‑centered design, rapid prototyping, product‑driven delivery models experiencestrong testing/automation practices, automated validation and monitoringtrack record driving operational excellence and continuousBenefits

competitive salarybonuses and benefitscompany-funded retirement contributiongenerous time-away and health benefits from day one, with the opportunity for flexible work options2-for-1 matching gifts for your charitable contributions and the opportunity to secure annual grants for the organizations you loveon-demand professional development resources that allow you to hone existing skills and learn new ones

jobs.title: Hiring: Digital Workplace Engineer in Kingston, NC
jobs.summary: Greetings from SRI Tech Solutions. SRI Tech Solutions Inc., an IT services and consulting firm with a focus on staffing, project solutions, and product development. Job Title: Digital Workplace Engineer Location: Kingston, NC Duration: 12 Months Job...
jobs.description:
Greetings from SRI Tech Solutions.

SRI Tech Solutions Inc., an IT services and consulting firm with a focus on staffing, project solutions, and product development. Job Title: Digital Workplace Engineer
Location: Kingston, NC
Duration: 12 Months Job Summary:We are seeking a skilled and proactive Digital Workplace Engineer to join our IT team at client's location in Kinston, NC. This role is responsible for the design, deployment, management, and support of all end-user computing devices, collaboration tools, and the overall digital employee experience. The ideal candidate will possess a strong foundation in end-user technology coupled with a solid understanding of networking principles, IT infrastructure components, and cybersecurity best practices. You will play a crucial role in ensuring the stability, security, and efficient operation of our digital workplace environment while collaborating closely with network, infrastructure, and security teams to enhance productivity and user satisfaction. You will also have to support the users adjust and adapt to the new solutions that will be deployed over the coming months. Key Responsibilities:
End-User Support & Troubleshooting:Provide timely and effective technical support for all desktop, laptop, mobile devices, and associated peripherals, including hardware, software, and collaboration tools.Diagnose and resolve complex technical issues related to operating systems (Windows, macOS, Linux), applications, and network connectivity within the digital workplace.Manage user accounts, permissions, and access rights in accordance with security policies, focusing on a seamless user experience. Digital Workplace Technology Management:Image, configure, and deploy new and replacement desktop, laptop, and mobile systems.Manage and maintain endpoint management solutions (e.g., SCCM, Intune, Jamf) for software distribution, patch management, inventory, and policy enforcement.Administer and support collaboration platforms (e.g., Microsoft 365, Teams, SharePoint, video conferencing solutions).Develop and maintain standard operating procedures (SOPs) for digital workplace support, deployment, and management.Network & Infrastructure Understanding:Troubleshoot basic network connectivity issues (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, Wi-Fi) impacting end-user devices and digital workplace services.Understand network topologies, VPN configurations, and cloud connectivity to assist in resolving user access problems.Collaborate with network and infrastructure teams on projects and incident resolution impacting the digital workplace and end-user services.Assist in documenting network and infrastructure configurations relevant to digital workplace support.Cybersecurity Focus:Implement and enforce endpoint security policies and configurations (e.g., antivirus, anti-malware, encryption, DLP, MFA) across the digital workplace.Respond to and assist in the remediation of security incidents involving end-user devices and accounts.Educate users on cybersecurity best practices and promote a security-aware culture.Ensure compliance with industry regulations (e.g., aerospace-specific, CMMC if applicable) and company security standards for all digital workplace technologies.Documentation & Reporting:Maintain accurate records of hardware inventory, software licenses, and support activities in the IT service management (ITSM) system.Create and update technical documentation, user guides, and knowledge base articles for digital workplace technologies.Generate reports on digital workplace performance, user satisfaction, and support metrics.Project & Collaboration:Participate in IT projects related to digital workplace upgrades, migrations, new technology implementations, and employee experience initiatives.Work collaboratively with other IT team members, business units, and vendors to deliver effective digital workplace solutions.Champion the adoption of new digital tools and practices to improve productivity and collaboration.
Education & Experience:Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field, OR equivalent practical experience.7 years of experience in a desktop support, endpoint engineering, or digital workplace role, preferably within a large enterprise or manufacturing environment.Technical Skills:
Proficiency in supporting and troubleshooting Windows (10/11) and macOS operating systems. iOS and Android experience is a plus.
Experience with endpoint management tools (e.g., Microsoft Intune, SCCM, Jamf).
Strong understanding of Active Directory, Azure AD, Group Policy, and identity and access management (IAM) concepts.
Experience with Microsoft 365 suite administration (Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, Exchange Online).Networking: Solid understanding of TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPN, and wireless networking concepts. Ability to perform basic network troubleshooting.Infrastructure: Familiarity with server virtualization (e.g., VMware, Hyper-V), cloud services (e.g., Azure, AWS), and general IT infrastructure components.Cybersecurity: Knowledge of endpoint security principles, antivirus/anti-malware solutions, data encryption, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and incident response basics. Familiarity with security frameworks (e.g., NIST, ISO 27001, CMMC) is a plus.Experience with scripting (e.g., PowerShell, Bash) for automation of digital workplace tasks is desirable.Familiarity with ITIL or other ITSM frameworks.Soft Skills:
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
Strong communication (written and verbal) and interpersonal skills, with an ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users.
Customer-focused with a dedication to providing a high-quality digital employee experience.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a collaborative environment.
Proactive, self-motivated, and eager to learn new technologies and improve processes.
Ability to manageDeliverables include but are not limited to:
Optimized Endpoint Deployment Process
Enhanced M365 Collaboration Guide
Endpoint Security Compliance Report
Knowledge Base Expansion
Deployment Plan and Instructions
Operating Model and Instructions
Training Plans and User Manuals SRI Tech Solutions is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, gender, religion, age, sexual orientation, national origin or citizenship status or ethnic origin, disability, marital status, veteran status, or any other occupationally irrelevant criteria.
