jobs.title: Senior Digital Workplace Technician - USDS
jobs.summary: Washington D.C. Regular R&D - Security Job ID: A15629 Responsibilities About the Team The IT Support team's mission is to empower our organization with innovative technology solutions that enhance productivity, streamline operations, and drive growth. The...
jobs.description:
Washington D.C.
Regular
R&D - Security
Job ID: A15629

Responsibilities

About the Team The IT Support team's mission is to empower our organization with innovative technology solutions that enhance productivity, streamline operations, and drive growth. The IT Support team partners with cross-functional teams across the organization to deliver reliable technical support, guidance, and solutions. We are committed to collaboration, service excellence, and making technology work seamlessly for our employees. Through collaboration and a commitment to excellence, we aim to make technology work seamlessly for our users, ultimately enabling them to focus on what matters most – delivering value to our organization and our customers. About the Role The Sr. Digital Workplace Technician would be providing general hardware and software to business users and working with stakeholders to remove friction and increase staff productivity through the use of AI and automation. Due to the nature of the technical work, the candidate will be required to provide resolutions to tickets and service requests from FTEs, BPOs, TPAs either onsite at a TT USDS JV office location or remote via Lark and if needed Google Chat. Tasks and Responsibilities: - Site Ownership: Serves as the technical lead for onsite and regional offices, overseeing site visits, office expansions - Executive Cross-Functional Partnership: Acts as a professional IT ambassador, partnering with Engineering, Product, and Operations to align regional technology with global corporate objectives and AI-driven workflows. - Elite Technical Support (Tier 1-3): Provides top-notch, white-glove support for both onsite and remote teams, acting as the final escalation point for complex hardware and software troubleshooting. - Modern A/V & Live Stream Engineering: Owns the end-to-end setup and support of sophisticated A/V environments. Provides hands-on expertise with Logitech conference systems, Apple Mac Mini/iPad room controllers, Crestron control systems, and Lightware signal management. - Standardization & Documentation: Authors technical standards, security policies, and "Golden Image" best practices for global deployment, ensuring consistency across all regional sites. - Enterprise Service Management: Partners with service owners to evaluate and deploy enterprise solutions. Maintains Admin-level expertise across both Google Workspace and Microsoft Office 365 environments. - Infrastructure & Data Integrity: Manages lifecycle, backups, and monitoring for critical office infrastructure. Directs technical input for enterprise projects, emphasizing "Infrastructure as Code" (IaC) principles. - AI-Driven Efficiency & Automation: Proactively identifies operational gaps and builds solutions using AI tools (Gemini, Claude, ChatGPT) and automation platforms (Zapier, DevinAI) to eliminate manual tasks and improve user experience. - Lifecycle & Procurement: Manages onsite supply chains and procurement of high-end technical assets, ensuring the office is equipped with the latest verified technology. - Security & Compliance Champion: Maintains a high degree of awareness regarding cyber/information security, ensuring onsite hardware and networks adhere to Zero Trust frameworks.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications - Advanced IT Foundation: Extensive direct user-facing support experience within a high-growth or FAANG/MAANG-equivalent environment. Expert-level troubleshooting across Windows OS, macOS, and Unix/Linux environments. - Education: College degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Computer Engineering, or a relevant technical discipline. - SaaS & Identity Mastery: Expert-level administration of Google Workspace and Microsoft Office 365. Deep familiarity with Google IAM, Active Directory, and Cloud-based identity providers. - UEM Expertise: Mastery of Omnissa Workspace One for multi-OS deployment (macOS, Windows, iOS, Android) and policy management. - Full-Stack Networking: Strong understanding of general networking (DNS, DHCP, SSL, OSI Model, TCP/IP) and hands-on familiarity with Cisco, Aruba, Palo Alto, and SDN technologies in MDF/IDF environments. - AI & LLM Proficiency: Demonstrated ability to utilize Gemini, Claude, ChatGPT, and DevinAI to write scripts, troubleshoot code, and automate workflows. Proficiency in Zapier for connecting disparate SaaS tools. Preferred Qualifications - Enterprise A/V Certifications: Relevant training or experience with Crestron programming or Lightware switching systems. - Advanced Networking/Security: Industry certifications such as CCNA, CCNP, MCSE, CompTIA Sec+, or CISSP. - Infrastructure Automation: Experience with Python, Bash, or PowerShell for automating system administration tasks. - Cloud Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with AWS, GCP, or Azure environments. - Virtualization: Familiarity with VMware Horizon VDI or similar cloud-desktop solutions. - Project Management: Proven experience managing large-scale IT office builds, moves, or global software migrations.

Job Information

【For Pay Transparency】 Compensation Description (Annually) - Washington, DC

The base salary range for this position in the selected city is $ 98640 - $ 163200 annually.

Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

Benefits may vary depending on the nature of employment and the country work location. Employees have day one access to medical, dental, and vision insurance, a 401(k) savings plan with company match, paid parental leave, short-term and long-term disability coverage, life insurance, wellbeing benefits, among others. Employees also receive 10 paid holidays per year, 10 paid sick days per year and 17 days of Paid Personal Time (prorated upon hire with increasing accruals by tenure).

The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefits programs at any time, with or without notice.

About USDS

TikTok USDS Joint Venture LLC is dedicated to the safety and security of millions of Americans who create, discover, and connect with what they love on the apps we operate. The Joint Venture has been established in compliance with the Executive Order signed by President Trump on September 25, 2025. Our foundation is a comprehensive data privacy and cybersecurity program we operate under defined safeguards to protect national security and secure U.S. user data, apps and the algorithm. We safeguard the U.S. content ecosystem, holding decision-making authority for trust and safety policies and moderation. USDS Joint Venture helps ensure Americans can continue to express their creativity, discover new hobbies and interests, and build thriving communities and businesses on a global scale.

On-site presence across teams allows the company to operate with greater speed, alignment, and agility — especially in areas like real-time decision-making, team development, and integrated execution. As such, the company is shifting from a hybrid work model to a fully in-person schedule up to 5 days a week.

Why Join Us

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.

We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion

TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

USDS Reasonable Accommodation

USDS is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at https://tinyurl.com/USDS-RA

jobs.title: Program Manager, Principal - Digital Workplace
jobs.summary: Requisition ID # 172386 Job Category: Project / Program Management Job Level: Manager/Principal Business Unit: Information Technology Work Type: Hybrid Job Location: Oakland Department Overview PG&Es Information Technology organization delivers secure,...
jobs.description:
Requisition ID # 172386

Job Category: Project / Program Management

Job Level: Manager/Principal

Business Unit: Information Technology

Work Type: Hybrid

Job Location: Oakland

Department Overview

PG&Es Information Technology organization delivers secure, reliable, and high-quality technology solutions that support business operations and coworker productivity.

Digital Workplace focuses on the technology experiences employees rely on every day - across office, field, and hybrid environments - ensuring tools, platforms, and services enable productivity, reliability, and a seamless user experience.

Executive Summary

This is a strategic, execution-focused program management role within PG&Es Digital Workplace organization. The Principal Program Manager owns the structure, cadence, and operating model that drives effective planning, prioritization, and portfolio management. This role exists to provide a single, enterprise-level planning and portfolio operating model for Digital Workplacereducing fragmentation, improving investment clarity, and enabling leadership to make timely, defensible decisions across competing priorities and constrained funding.

This role runs the planning and portfolio system that turns strategy into a funded, executable plan, ensuring investments are aligned, sequenced, and clearly understood by leadership.

The ideal candidate is a strong operator who brings structure to ambiguity and translates complex inputs into clear, defensible insights and actionable plans.

Position Summary

Digital Workplace is seeking a Principal Program Manager to lead portfolio planning and operational alignment across the organization.

This role is responsible for building and operating the systems, cadences, and reporting that enable Business Planning Development (BPD), portfolio prioritization, and executive decision-making. It brings structure and consistency to planning inputs, transforming fragmented data and competing priorities into a cohesive, defensible portfolio plan.

Working closely with Enterprise Architecture and domain SMEs, this role ensures planning decisions are informed, aligned, and executable - effectively bridging strategy to delivery through disciplined portfolio management.

This is a high-impact role operating across multiple teams and stakeholders, driving alignment, accountability, and visibility across the Digital Workplace portfolio.

PG&E is providing the full salary/pay range for this position. The actual amount paid to an individual will be based on multiple factors, including, but not limited to, internal equity, specific skills, education, licenses or certifications, experience, market value, and geographic location. The range to reasonably expect will be around the minimum and the midpoint $144,000 - $194,000. The final decision will be made on a case-by-case basis related to the factors above. This job is also eligible to participate in PG&Es discretionary incentive compensation programs.

Bay Area Min: $ 144,000

Bay Area Max: $ 244,000

This position is hybrid, working from your remote office at our Oakland Headquarters based on business need. The assigned work location will be within the PG&E Service Territory.

Job Responsibilities

Lead and operate the Business Planning Development (BPD) process for Digital Workplace, including intake, prioritization, and delivery of the final funded portfolio planMaintain a single portfolio backlog of initiatives, investments, and lifecycle needs, ensuring consistent structure, dependencies, and statusEstablish and manage planning cadences and governance forums, driving alignment on priorities, tradeoffs, and decision timelinesPartner with Enterprise Architecture and SMEs to develop business cases and decision options, articulating cost, value, and tradeoffsConsolidate inputs across teams into an integrated portfolio view and multi-year roadmap, aligning priorities, dependencies, and constraints for leadership decision-makingTrack portfolio performance and provide clear visibility into progress, risks, and delivery confidenceBalance portfolio demand with delivery capacity by sequencing initiatives to optimize throughput, manage interdependencies, and avoid over-commitment or change saturation.Analyze technology contracts and vendor performance, including license utilization and cost-to-value outcomes, and develop optimization recommendationsTranslate technical, operational, and experience signals (voice of coworker, CSAT) into executive-ready insights, partnering with the Coworker Technology Council (CWTC) to orchestrate feedback and ensure inputs are integrated into roadmap and portfolio decisionFrame complex portfolio data, scenarios, and tradeoffs into clear decision options and recommendations for senior leadership and governance forumsAssess cumulative change impact across the portfolio, partnering with change and adoption leads to ensure initiatives are sequenced in alignment with coworker readiness and operational stabilityDevelop and maintain portfolio reporting, ensuring leadership has accurate, actionable visibility into investments, priorities, and outcomesFacilitate prioritization and tradeoff discussions, ensuring alignment to enterprise objectives, constraints, and funding realitiesAct as the central liaison across Digital Workplace, Enterprise Architecture, SMEs, Finance, and leadership, driving alignment and coordinationIdentify and proactively address risks and blockers impacting planning timelines and execution readinessMaintain portfolio governance and control practices, including decision tracking, dependencies, and artifact integrityDrive continuous improvement of planning, prioritization, and portfolio management processes to increase efficiency, transparency, and delivery effectivenessQualifications

Minimum:

Bachelors degree or equivalent experience10 years of experience in program management, portfolio operations, business operations, or strategic planningDesired:

Experience in large-scale or regulated environmentsProven experience leading enterprise planning cycles and cross-functional coordinationStrong experience developing business cases, financial analysis, and decision frameworksDemonstrated ability to produce data-driven insights and executive-level reportingExcellent communication, facilitation, and stakeholder management skillsBackground in portfolio planning, financial management, or technology strategy supportExperience driving process improvements and operational disciplineCore Skills & Attributes

jobs.title: Director, Digital Workplace Platforms
jobs.summary: Are you a visionary leader with a passion for driving digital transformation? Do you have a deep understanding of technology and a track record of successfully implementing innovative solutions? If so, Applied Materials has an exciting opportunity for you...
jobs.description:
Are you a visionary leader with a passion for driving digital transformation? Do you have a deep understanding of technology and a track record of successfully implementing innovative solutions? If so, Applied Materials has an exciting opportunity for you to join our team as the Director of Digital Workplace Platforms. In this role, you will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive strategy to enhance our digital workplace and drive productivity, collaboration, and engagement across our global organization. We are seeking a dynamic individual with strong leadership skills, excellent communication abilities, and a demonstrated ability to manage complex initiatives. If you are ready to make a significant impact and shape the future of our digital workplace, we encourage you to apply today.

Develop and implement a comprehensive strategy for digital workplace platforms, in alignment with the company's overall goals and objectives.
Drive digital transformation across the organization, utilizing your strong understanding of technology and innovative thinking.
Lead and manage complex initiatives to enhance our digital workplace, including identifying and implementing new technologies, systems, and processes.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure the successful integration of digital workplace solutions.
Provide visionary leadership to inspire and motivate team members towards achieving digital workplace goals.
Communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels of the organization, including executives, to gain buy-in and support for digital workplace initiatives.
Identify and assess potential risks and challenges related to digital workplace platforms, and develop mitigation strategies.
Monitor and analyze data to measure the effectiveness and impact of digital workplace solutions, and use this information to continually improve and optimize processes.
Stay informed about industry trends and advancements in digital workplace technology, and make recommendations for incorporating them into our strategy.
Foster a culture of collaboration, engagement, and productivity within the digital workplace, and ensure alignment with company values and culture.
Provide guidance and mentorship to team members, promoting their growth and development within the organization.
Collaborate with HR and other departments to ensure digital workplace solutions support employee needs and enhance the overall employee experience.

Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

jobs.description:
Overview Senior Director, Digital Workplace Experience — Dale WorkForce Solutions. Responsibilities Lead the development and implementation of the digital workplace strategy, focusing on user delight, adoption, and a digital environment where technology enables success. Oversee a budget of up to $20M and the integration and optimization of collaboration tools (e.g., Microsoft 365, Teams, SharePoint), endpoint management, AV solutions, and digital assistant technologies. Stay ahead of emerging trends in digital employee experience, workplace automation, and AI-driven productivity tools. Lead a team of 15 with external partners, shaping an integrated digital solutions roadmap for a dynamic, hybrid work environment. Collaborate with functional leaders to develop and maintain digital experience roadmaps that address current needs and incorporate emerging technologies. Drive user adoption, improve digital engagement, and ensure technology serves as a key enabler of organizational success. Ensure in-office AV technologies are reliable and provide a modern, frictionless workplace experience. Identify ways to simplify the integration of physical and technology environments and support hybrid work arrangements. Strategic Communications & Change Management Lead strategic communications that emphasize the value of digital experience management to foster a digitally savvy workforce. Lead enterprise-wide adoption strategies, training, and communications to ensure successful rollouts and sustained use of digital workplace solutions. Cross-Functional & Security Partner with HR, Facilities, Security, and IT to align initiatives with the employee lifecycle in a frictionless manner. Ensure all digital workplace solutions meet security requirements and continuously improve the digital workplace to delight customers. People Leadership Drive effective performance management, professional growth, and an inclusive work environment; motivate and align the team with organizational goals. The role partners with leaders to co-create roadmaps that meet current needs and anticipate future technologies shaping the future of work. #J-18808-Ljbffr

jobs.description:
Enterprise IT Systems Engineer

Support enterprise-level IT infrastructure and services for mission-critical Department of Defense ( DoD ) environments. Display expertise across a wide range of Micro sof t, VMware, and cloud technologies, with a strong focus on secure, scalable, and resilient system design and support. Administer, configure, and support Micro sof t Active Directory, Group Policy , and Certificate Authority ( CA ) or Public Key Infrastructure ( PKI ) . Manage enterprise backup and D isa ster Recovery (DR) solutions ensuring data resiliency and compliance with DoD standards. Design and maintain Storage Area Networks ( SAN ) and Hyper-Converged Infrastructure ( HCI ) for scalable storage and compute needs. Implement and maintain Data-At-Rest ( DAR ) encryption solutions using Micro sof t BitLocker. Ensure systems align with the DoD Cloud Computing Security Requirements Guide ( SRG ) . Administer and support Micro sof t Exchange, SharePoint, SQL databases, and web hosting. Deliver secure, high-performance capabilities, including VoIP, VDI, mobile computing, and video teleconferencing. Manage and optimize VMware virtual infrastructure and container technologies such as Kubernetes and Docker. Design and maintain solutions within Micro sof t Office 365, leveraging IaaS, PaaS, and other cloud-based models.

12+ years of experience with enterprise systems engineering in a DoD or secure environmentExperience with Micro sof t enterprise technologies, virtualization platforms, or cloud servicesExperience with SAN, HCI, and backup or DR architecturesExperience supporting deployable or tactical IT environmentsExperience with DoD cybersecurity compliance, including RMF or STIGsTS / SCI clearanceHS diploma or GEDAbility to obtain a DoD 8570.11 IAT Level II Certification, such as CompTIA Security+ CE, CCNA-Security, GSEC, or SSCP Certification, within 30 days of start date

Experience applying core systems engineering concepts, processes, practices, and procedures in technical assignmentsExperience across enterprise-level platforms encompassing servers, storage, networking, sof tware, and application servicesExperience collaborating and coordinating with senior-level stakeholders and organizational leadersAbility to mentor junior team members, fostering their growth and technical excellencePossession of excellent collaboration and communication skillsBachelor's degree in CS, IT, Engineering, or a related fieldAdvanced Cybersecurity Certification such as CASP+ or CISSP Certification

Applicants selected will be subject to a security investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements for access to classified information ; TS/SCI clearance is required.

At Booz Allen, we celebrate your contributions, provide you with opportunities and choices, and support your total well-being. Our offerings include health, life, disability, financial, and retirement benefits, as well as paid leave, professional development, tuition assistance, work-life programs, and dependent care. Our recognition awards program acknowledges employees for exceptional performance and superior demonstration of our values. Full-time and part-time employees working at least 20 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in Booz Allen's benefit programs. Individuals that do not meet the threshold are only eligible for select offerings, not inclusive of health benefits. We encourage you to learn more about our total benefits by visiting the Resource page on our Careers site and reviewing Our Employee Benefits page.

Salary at Booz Allen is determined by various factors, including but not limited to location, the individual's particular combination of education, knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience, as well as contract-specific affordability and organizational requirements. The projected compensation range for this position is $86,900.00 to $198,000.00 (annualized USD). The estimate displayed represents the typical salary range for this position and is just one component of Booz Allen's total compensation package for employees. This posting will close within 90 days from the Posting Date.

As part of the hiring process, we will ask you to complete an identity verification process that leverages advanced biometrics and artificial intelligence to ensure authenticity and protect against identity fraud. You are expected to be on camera during interviews and assessments. We reserve the right to take your picture to verify your identity and prevent fraud.

AI is a part of our daily work at Booz Allen, and we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI tools. However, we want to ensure a fair candidate process based on own skills and knowledge. As part of this commitment, the use of artificial intelligence (AI) or other tools to assist with responses during interviews (whether in-person or virtual) is prohibited .

Our people-first culture prioritizes the benefits of collaboration, whether it occurs in person or virtually. To support engagement and effective communication, employees working virtually are generally expected to have their cameras on during meetings.

: If this position is listed as remote, there may still be occasions when you are required to work in person at a Booz Allen or customer facility.: If this position is listed as hybrid, you will be expected to work from a Booz Allen facility frequently, in alignment with leadership expectations and the needs of the role. You may also be required to work from or visit a customer facility.: If this position is listed as onsite, work will primarily be performed at a Booz Allen office or customer facility, where employees will collaborate directly with colleagues and customers as required by the role.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local, or international law.

jobs.title: IT Systems Engineer- Level II
jobs.company: Orthopaedic & Spine Center of the Rockies
jobs.summary: Description: Are you passionate about serving patients in an inspiring work environment alongside talented people? Get moving with OCR! With over 50 years in business, we are the premier provider of orthopedic care in Colorado, Wyoming, and Nebraska. Our...
jobs.description:
Description:
Are you passionate about serving patients in an inspiring work environment alongside talented people? Get moving with OCR! With over 50 years in business, we are the premier provider of orthopedic care in Colorado, Wyoming, and Nebraska. Our organization continues to grow, and we are looking for individuals who share our mission.

The Systems Engineer- Level II is responsible for the maintenance, configuration, and reliable operation of OCR computer systems. This position will install hardware and software to end users, manage various systems and ensure minimal downtime and efficient operations. They also will participate in system testing and recommendations for system improvements.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

Performs full lifecycle of installation, management, maintenance, and disposal of workstations and laptops.
Manages users in Active Directory and Microsoft Azure.
Manage Microsoft E365 tenant account, including user account license assignments, Exchange Online mailboxes, Teams, OneDrive and SharePoint files, and Intune.
Designs, tests and implements Active Directory Group Purchasing Organizations (GPOs) in coordination with System Architect.
Recommends and implements cybersecurity policies and practices, in coordination with System Architect.
Performs system maintenance and patching regularly within the respective change windows.
Maintains adequate clone images and spare workstations for rapid deployment and replacement.
Installs and configures hardware in various company locations; revises processes and procedures as needed.
Participates in the IT change management process, and adheres to it.
Maintains system and application diagrams and documentation.
Participates in daily helpdesk ticket resolution.
Participates in IT on-call rotation.
Participates in tier 1 support for Epic electronic health record
Other duties as assigned.
Requirements:

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in computer science, information systems, or related field, required.
Two years’ experience with active directory management.
Two years’ experience with Microsoft operating systems and Microsoft Office suites.
Experience with software maintenance and testing.
Additional education may be substituted for experience.

Physical Requirements

The work environment is a typical medical office environment. The employee must be able to complete their work satisfactorily in an environment where there are significant distractions, including staff, patients, and vendors walking through and conversing, telephones ringing, conversations carrying over, loud noises, and interruptions to answer questions from others.
While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit and use hands to finger, handle, or feel objects, tools, or controls. The employee frequently is required to talk or hear. The employee is frequently required to stand; walk; reach with hands and arms, and stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl.
The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to 25 pounds and should do so in a sound and safe manner. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus.

Benefits:

Medical, Dental, & Vision coverage
Life and AD&D Insurance
Short- and Long-term disability coverage
Retirement savings and profit sharing plan participation
Employee Assistance Program
Paid Holidays & Paid Time Off
Company-sponsored events
Annual merit increases

**Benefit eligibility is dependent on employment status, and a waiting period may apply

Orthopaedic & Spine Center of the Rockies (OCR) is an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibits discrimination or harassment of any kind. OCR is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions are based on business needs, job requirements and individual experience and qualifications, without regard to a person's race, color, age, sex, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, sexual orientation, religion, creed, national origin, the presence of any physical or mental disability, or status as a disabled veteran, recently separated veteran, other protected veteran, or Armed Forces service medal veteran, or any other protected status.

Applications will be accepted until the position is filled; to receive full consideration, please apply by July 18, 2026

jobs.title: Modern Workplace Engineer
jobs.summary: The Modern Workplace Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, and supporting a secure, cloud-first end-user computing environment built on Microsoft technologies. This role focuses on Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Azure, and Microsoft...
jobs.description:
The Modern Workplace Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, and supporting a secure, cloud-first end-user computing environment built on Microsoft technologies. This role focuses on Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Azure, and Microsoft Entra ID to deliver a modern, scalable, and user-centric workplace experience across Windows, mobile, and cloud platforms. The engineer partners closely with security, infrastructure, and end-user support teams to standardize device management, strengthen identity and access controls, and ensure seamless, secure productivity. The position plays a key role in advancing Zero Trust principles, improving operational efficiency, and enabling continuous improvement of the organization's modern workplace capabilities.

REPONSIBILITIES:

Design, implement, and operate Microsoft Intune and Windows Autopilot for device provisioning, configuration, compliance, patching, and application deployment across Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android platforms.Administer Microsoft Entra ID, including users, groups, devices, RBAC, MFA, SSO, and Conditional Access policies aligned with Zero Trust and secure access to Microsoft 365 and third-party applications.Implement endpoint security baselines, support Defender and compliance initiatives, ensure regulatory alignment, and participate in audits, incident response, and continuous security improvements.Support Azure-based services that enable the modern workplace, collaborate on hybrid and cloud-native identity/device strategies, and monitor configurations for performance, security, and cost optimization.Provide advanced troubleshooting for modern workplace issues, partner with service desk and engineering teams, and resolve complex endpoint, identity, and access-related incidents.Develop and maintain technical documentation, standards, and runbooks; evaluate new Microsoft workplace features; and recommend enhancements to improve user experience and operational efficiency.

REQUIREMENTS:

Bachelor's degree in Information Technology or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and hands-on enterprise experience supporting modern workplace technologies.Hands-on enterprise experience with Microsoft Intune for device and application managementProven experience deploying and managing Windows Autopilot for device provisioning and lifecycle managementStrong working knowledge of Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), including users, groups, devices, and Conditional AccessExperience supporting Microsoft Azure and cloud-based workplace servicesDeep understanding of Windows 11, modern device management, and endpoint security conceptsStrong troubleshooting, documentation, and communication skills in an enterprise environmentMicrosoft certifications such as MD-102, SC-300, AZ-104, or MS-102 are preferred

COMPENSATION: The national average salary range is $120,000-$130,000. The salary may be higher or lower depending on internal equity and years of experience.

BENEFITS: Medical Insurance, Dental Insurance, Vision Insurance, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Accounts, Life insurance, Disability Insurance, Paid Parental Leave, 401(k) Plan, Paid Days Off/Vacation, Firm Paid Holidays.

jobs.description:
About the position

The Modern Workplace Engineer Level I engineer supports Microsoft 365 environments for our Defense Industrial Base customers. This individual is responsible for delivering secure, compliant, and efficient modern workplace experiences using the Microsoft 365 E5 suite. This role will focus on both the ongoing operations of managed services as well as project-based onboarding and adoption. Role & Responsibilities: End User Productivity Support Provide Tier I support for Microsoft 365 applications, including: Exchange Online Microsoft Teams SharePoint Online OneDrive for Business Microsoft Office Apps (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.) Escalate support requests as necessary to Level II engineers. Troubleshoot issues related to Microsoft 365 access, synchronization, and collaboration. Identity & Access Support Configure and maintain Conditional Access, passwordless authentication, and identity protection policies in Microsoft Entra ID. Support hybrid identity scenarios and secure guest access across Microsoft 365 services. Security & Compliance Tasks Assist with reviewing and applying sensitivity labels, retention policies, and data loss prevention (DLP) rules within Microsoft Purview (Compliance Center). Monitor alerts in Microsoft Defender for Office 365 and escalate issues per defined procedures. Service Delivery & Operations Respond to service requests and incidents in alignment with SLAs and internal escalation paths. Perform basic configuration and policy updates using Microsoft 365 Admin Center and Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune). Follow internal processes (such as change controls) for making changes in customer environments. Document technical issues, solutions, and recurring patterns in internal knowledge base systems. Customer Engagement Work directly with customer stakeholders, primarily technical contacts, to resolve routine issues and implement M365 changes. Participate in onboarding activities for new users, sites, or departments within existing managed customers. Team Collaboration Coordinate with higher-level engineers to escalate complex issues or contribute to larger projects. Participate in regular team syncs and ongoing training sessions to stay current on Microsoft 365 features and changes. Coordinate with Endpoint Engineering, Security Operations, and Azure Engineering teams to resolve issues.

Responsibilities

End User Productivity Support Provide Tier I support for Microsoft 365 applicationsIdentity & Access Support Configure and maintain Conditional Access, passwordless authentication, and identity protection policies in Microsoft Entra ID.Security & Compliance Tasks Assist with reviewing and applying sensitivity labels, retention policies, and data loss prevention (DLP) rules within Microsoft Purview (Compliance Center).Service Delivery & Operations Respond to service requests and incidents in alignment with SLAs and internal escalation paths.Customer Engagement Work directly with customer stakeholders, primarily technical contacts, to resolve routine issues and implement M365 changes.Team Collaboration Coordinate with higher-level engineers to escalate complex issues or contribute to larger projects.Requirements

1–2 years of IT support experience, preferably with a managed service provider (MSP) or in a customer-facing technical role.Working knowledge of Microsoft 365 administration portals (Admin Center, Teams Admin Center, Exchange Admin Center).Strong troubleshooting skills and attention to detail.Excellent written and verbal communication skills.Ability to work independently while following defined processes and procedures administering Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Exchange Online, Microsoft Teams, Microsoft 365 Apps, sensitivity labels, Microsoft 365 Defender, and Active Directory.Familiarity with remote management and monitoring (RMM) tools and patch management tools.U.S. Citizenship required (due to work with defense contractors and ITAR-regulated customers).Nice-to-haves

Experience with Zero Trust implementation principles in Microsoft 365 environments.Familiarity with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and its integration with Defender for Office 365.Familiarity with basic security and compliance concepts related to CMMC or NIST 800-171.Hands-on experience with Microsoft Purview compliance solutions.Experience working in regulated industries or classified environments.Microsoft 365 Certified: Fundamentals (MS-900)Microsoft 365 Certified: Enterprise Administrator Expert (MS-102)Microsoft 365 Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator Associate (MD-102)CompTIA Security+ (especially valued for work in DIB environments)

jobs.title: Engineering - Workplace Engineering - Cloud Platform - M365 and Mobile Lead Engi
jobs.summary: Your Impact At Goldman Sachs, Workplace Engineering is at the forefront of driving our firm's adoption of cutting-edge technologies for productivity, communications, and collaboration. As a Senior Technical Vice President, you will play a pivotal...
jobs.description:
Your Impact

At Goldman Sachs, Workplace Engineering is at the forefront of driving our firm's adoption of cutting-edge technologies for productivity, communications, and collaboration. As a Senior Technical Vice President, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping the strategic direction, architecture, and operational excellence of our Microsoft 365 platforms. Your vision and leadership will directly influence how our global workforce interacts, collaborates, and innovates, ensuring robust, secure, and scalable solutions that underpin our business objectives and client engagement. You will lead a high-performing engineering organization, driving innovation in web, mobile, social, collaboration, and user experience, fundamentally changing the way we operate across the firm.

Role Summary

This role is a Microsoft 365 Engineer with a core focus on Microsoft 365 engineering, Intune (mobile application and device management), M365 platform readiness, extensibility, security, and enterprise-scale adoption. We are seeking a visionary and experienced technical leader to serve as our Senior Technical Vice President, overseeing the strategic planning, engineering, and governance of our enterprise-wide Microsoft 365 ecosystem. This role demands a deep technical understanding of M365 services, coupled with exceptional leadership capabilities to guide global teams, define architectural standards, and drive the adoption of advanced features, including AI-powered capabilities like Copilot, the Teams suite, and the Power Platform. You will be instrumental in collaborating with senior product owners, architects, compliance, and risk teams to deliver transformative solutions that meet the stringent demands of a global financial institution, ensuring seamless integration across the broader Microsoft ecosystem including Windows 365.

Responsibilities

* Define and execute the long-term strategic vision and roadmap for all Microsoft 365 platforms and services, ensuring alignment with overall firm objectives, technological advancements, and regulatory requirements.

* Lead, mentor, and develop a global team of highly skilled engineers and technical managers, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement.

* Provide executive oversight for the design, architecture, and implementation of complex, enterprise-grade Microsoft 365 solutions, ensuring scalability, security, resilience, and seamless integration with existing firm-wide systems, including Windows 365 and the Power Platform.

* Establish and enforce robust governance frameworks, security policies, and compliance standards for all Microsoft 365 services, ensuring adherence to internal policies and external regulatory mandates within a highly regulated financial services environment.

* Collaborate extensively with senior product owners, enterprise architects, engineering leads, compliance, risk, and business unit leaders to understand strategic requirements, manage expectations, and drive successful outcomes for critical initiatives.

* Drive the evaluation, pilot, and strategic adoption of new Microsoft 365 features, emerging technologies, and AI-powered capabilities (e.g., Copilot, Teams, Power Platform) to enhance productivity, collaboration, and user experience across the firm.

* Oversee the operational, performance, and incident management processes for Microsoft 365 environments, serving as a primary executive escalation point for critical issues and ensuring rapid resolution and root cause analysis.

Basic Qualifications

* 12-15+ years of progressive experience in enterprise-level IT, with at least 10 years specifically focused on Microsoft 365 platforms and services in a significant leadership capacity within a large, complex organization.

* Proven track record of successfully leading, managing, and developing large, geographically dispersed technical teams, including managers and individual contributors.

* Demonstrated ability to define, articulate, and execute complex technology strategies and

roadmaps for critical enterprise collaboration platforms.

* Deep architectural and engineering understanding of the entire Microsoft 365 suite, including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Teams, OneDrive, Azure AD, Intune, Purview, Windows 365, and the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI), as well as advanced security/compliance features.

* Extensive experience designing, implementing, and managing Microsoft 365 solutions within stringent security, governance, and regulatory frameworks, preferably in the financial services industry.

* Exceptional executive-level communication, presentation, and negotiation skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts and strategic initiatives to diverse audiences, including senior leadership and non-technical stakeholders.

* Superior analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities, capable of navigating highly complex technical, organizational, and business challenges.

Preferred Qualifications

* Hands-on experience with large-scale Microsoft 365 Copilot deployments, Power Platform integrations, custom integrations, and advanced automation strategies for M365 management and operations.

* Strong understanding of broader cloud architecture principles (Azure, AWS, GCP) and experience managing hybrid cloud environments.

* Relevant industry certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Microsoft 365 Certified: Enterprise Administrator Expert, or similar.

* Prior experience in a senior technology leadership role within a global financial institution, with a strong understanding of industry-specific challenges and regulations.

* Proficiency in advanced PowerShell scripting, Azure Automation, and other automation frameworks for M365 management and operations.

Salary Range The expected base salary for this Seattle, Washington, United States-based position is $150000-$250000. In addition, you may be eligible for a discretionary bonus if you are an active employee as of fiscal year-end.

Benefits Goldman Sachs is committed to providing our people with valuable and competitive benefits and wellness offerings, as it is a core part of providing a strong overall employee experience. jobs.description:
Modern Workplace Cloud Integrated Infrastructure Engineer III-AI & Engineering

AI & Engineering leverages innovative engineering capabilities to build, deploy, and operate integrated/verticalized sector solutions in software, data, AI, network, and hybrid cloud infrastructure. These solutions are powered by engineering for business advantage, transforming mission-critical operations. We enable clients to stay ahead with the latest advancements by transforming engineering teams and modernizing technology & data platforms.

Our delivery models are tailored to meet each client's unique requirements.

Recruiting for this role ends on 06/09/2026.

Work you'll do
Our Hybrid Cloud Infrastructure offering delivers specialized engineering capabilities to design, implement, manage, and operate hybrid cloud environments. We modernize networks and AI infrastructure from the core to the edge and develop new infrastructure and device services to help clients stay ahead with the latest technology advancements.

The Modern Workplace Consultant strives to build an end user experience that enables users to work independently and efficiently adopting cutting edge technologies.

Our Modern
Workplace team guide our clients through multi-year journeys from the case for change through implementation across all dimensions of workplace services. We help our clients optimize their workplace experience through collaborative tools that increase productivity and allow seamless communication and collaborations amongst teams. These include transformations for BYOD, mobility, desktop delivery, collaboration, end user experience management, user support environments, Microsoft 365 Copilot implementations, Power Platform development, and virtual desktop infrastructure.

Our Team
Join our AI & Engineering team by transforming technology platforms, driving innovation, and helping make a significant impact on our clients' success. You'll collaborate with talented professionals, reimagining and re-engineering operations and processes that are critical to business. Your contributions can help clients improve financial performance, accelerate new digital ventures, and fuel growth through innovation.

Ability to travel 50%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve

jobs.description:
Description
Our customer is seeking a proactive and eager-to-learn Endpoint Support Specialist to join our team in Chicago. This role is crucial for providing direct, in-person technical support to our users at our "solution cafe" and ensuring smooth IT operations. While the daily volume is low, this position offers significant opportunity for growth into higher-level engineering tasks for a motivated individual.

Key Responsibilities: Provide hands-on, in-person technical support to end-users at the solution cafe, addressing a variety of hardware and software issues. Troubleshoot and resolve Tier 2/3 hardware, software, and operating system problems. Manage and resolve IT tickets that require physical intervention or on-site support.

Assist with device deployments using Intune and Autopilot, following established processes. Support a predominantly Lenovo Windows environment (99%), with occasional support for Mac devices. Provide support for the Office 365 environment.

Maintain a professional and presentable demeanor, as the office is occasionally used by executive leadership.

Required Skills &

Experience: 2-3 years of experience in a corporate IT support or help desk environment. Strong troubleshooting skills across hardware, software, and various operating systems.

Experience supporting Microsoft Office 365. Demonstrated ability to learn new applications and technologies quickly. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with a customer-service oriented approach. A proactive attitude, eagerness to learn, and a desire for professional growth.

Desired
Skills (for long-term growth): Familiarity with Intune and Autopilot beyond basic deployment processes. Interest in developing skills in IT engineering areas such as packaging, PowerShell scripting, and Azure.

Experience with conference room systems (e.g., Crestron, Neat) is a plus.

Skills
Technical support, O365, Windows 11, Autopilot, InTune

Top Skills Details
Technical support,O365,Windows 11

Additional Skills & Qualifications

Excellent customer service and problem solving skills

Experience Level
Intermediate Level
Job Type & Location

This is a Contract position based out of Chicago, IL.

Eligibility requirements apply to some benefits and may depend on your job classification and length of employment.

Medical, dental & vision

Critical Illness, Accident, and Hospital

401(k) Retirement Plan - Pre-tax and Roth post-tax contributions available

Life Insurance (Voluntary Life & AD&D for the employee and dependents)

Short and long-term disability

Health Spending Account (HSA)
Transportation benefits
Employee Assistance Program

This is a fully onsite position in Chicago,IL.

This position is anticipated to close on Jun 15, 2026.

Overview:

Our client functions as the IT team for their customers. They will be hiring a Cloud Administrator 1 with strong PowerShell scripting or coding skills and strong Microsoft 365 skills.

This role is responsible for implementing, configuring and supporting technology solutions throughout the organization with a focus on Azure and Microsoft 365 solutions.

In the first 90 days, the expectation is to work primarily onsite in Vancouver, WA. Once you get up to speed, 2-3 days remote expectation is ideal.

The target customers are small businesses (100 employees or less) in the northwest, every customer is word of mouth, leadership really cares about customers feeling like they've got an IT partner they can trust.

Selling points:

A candidate who enjoys being an IT generalist tend to be the best fit. They are not bleeding edge with technology, but everyone makes an impact on the company, you'll get to influence how the company evolves and directly help small businesses in the northwest. The work culture values structure and documentation, they are 100% ticket-driven organization.

jobs.title: IT - Sr Staff Systems Engineer (Active Directory, Powershell, and Windows/O365 Infrastructure Administration)
jobs.summary: At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology. Job Summary: We are looking for a skilled detail-oriented specialist to manage and secure Cadence’s Active Directory environment, Windows server...
jobs.description:
At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.
Job Summary:

We are looking for a skilled detail-oriented specialist to manage and secure Cadence’s Active Directory environment, Windows server administration including OS hardening, vulnerability, patch management. Candidate with solid background in Windows based environment and Active Directory architecture, identity, and access management, and securing directory services against evolving threats.

Job Description:

Provide day-to-day administration and support of Microsoft Active Directory environments (version 2016/209 and above).

Version upgrade of Windows Active Directory. Setting up Read/Write and Read only domain controllers.

Maintain and troubleshoot Group Policy Objects (GPOs) to ensure secure and consistent configurations.

Setup and manager authentication Policies / Silos, gMSA.

Work with DNS, DHCP, and other core services closely integrated with AD.

Perform regular audits and cleanup of AD objects to maintain a secure and efficient environment.

Monitor and troubleshoot authentication issues including Kerberos, NTLM, and account lockouts.

Ensure the security and integrity of the Active Directory infrastructure.

Conduct regular audits of AD objects, group memberships, and permissions to identify and remediate misconfigurations or security risks.

Administer and maintain Windows Server environments. Implement and maintain security hardening across Windows Servers. Version 2016/1029 and above

Analyze security bulletins, prioritize patches based on risk, and deploy patches.

Document changes, configurations, and standard operating procedures.

Proficiency in PowerShell or other scripting languages for task automation.

Requirements:

10+ years of experience in support of Active directory and windows server environments.

Proficiency with PowerShell scripting and other scripting tools for auditing, automation, and remediation.

In-depth knowledge of Active Directory, Kerberos, GPOs, DNS, and DHCP.

Strong understanding of Microsoft Windows Server environments and administration.

Experience with a Microsoft O365 environment is a plus.

Familiarity with security best practices for hybrid identity environments.

Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills.

Good written and verbal Communications Skills.

Education:

Must have a Bachelors Degree in Engineering/Technical field.

Job Location:

This is an onsite position. Must be able to work in-person from Cadence’s San Jose office; Monday through Thursday.

The annual salary range for California is $136,500 to $253,500. You may also be eligible to receive incentive compensation: bonus, equity, and benefits. Sales positions generally offer a competitive On Target Earnings (OTE) incentive compensation structure. Please note that the salary range is a guideline and compensation may vary based on factors such as qualifications, skill level, competencies and work location. Our benefits programs include: paid vacation and paid holidays, 401(k) plan with employer match, employee stock purchase plan, a variety of medical, dental and vision plan options, and more.

We’re doing work that matters. Help us solve what others can’t.

jobs.summary: Introduction Do you have the career opportunities as a Senior Systems Engineer you want with your current employer? We have an exciting opportunity for you to join HCA Healthcare which is part of the nation's leading provider of healthcare services, HCA...
jobs.description:
Introduction
Do you have the career opportunities as a Senior Systems Engineer you want with your current employer? We have an exciting opportunity for you to join HCA Healthcare which is part of the nation's leading provider of healthcare services, HCA Healthcare.

Benefits
HCA Healthcare offers a total rewards package that supports the health, life, career and retirement of our colleagues. The available plans and programs include:

Comprehensive medical coverage that covers many common services at no cost or for a low copay. Plans include prescription drug and behavioral health coverage as well as free telemedicine services and free AirMed medical transportation.
Additional options for dental and vision benefits, life and disability coverage, flexible spending accounts, supplemental health protection plans (accident, critical illness, hospital indemnity), auto and home insurance, identity theft protection, legal counseling, long-term care coverage, moving assistance, pet insurance and more.
Free counseling services and resources for emotional, physical and financial wellbeing

401(k) Plan with a 100% match on 3% to 9% of pay (based on years of service)

Employee Stock Purchase Plan with 10% off HCA Healthcare stock

Family support through fertility and family building benefits with Progyny and adoption assistance.

Referral services for child, elder and pet care, home and auto repair, event planning and more

Consumer discounts through Abenity and Consumer Discounts

Retirement readiness, rollover assistance services and preferred banking partnerships

Education assistance (tuition, student loan, certification support, dependent scholarships)

Colleague recognition program

Time Away From Work Program (paid time off, paid family leave, long- and short-term disability coverage and leaves of absence)

Employee Health Assistance Fund that offers free employee-only coverage to full-time and part-time colleagues based on income.

Learn more about Employee Benefits

Note: Eligibility for benefits may vary by location.

Our teams are a committed, caring group of colleagues. Do you want to work as a(an) Senior Systems Engineer where your passion for creating positive patient interactions is valued? If you are dedicated to caring for the well-being of others, this could be your next opportunity. We want your knowledge and expertise!

Job Summary and Qualifications

Job Summary: Primary Purpose of the job

The Enterprise Endpoint Services Senior Systems Engineer plays a critical role in delivering a scalable, high-performing software management platform for over 300,000 workstations across HCA. This position focuses on automating enterprise software delivery using PowerShell and System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM), ensuring consistent application deployment, compliance, and operational efficiency. This position also plays a key role in helping the team to support the SCCM infrastructure environment.

This person must be a positive, self-starter; possessing strong communication skills effectively focused on improving our customers' experience. They must possess an advanced ability to troubleshoot and dissect complex problems with strong communication skills that connect with all customer levels while collaboratively working with many other IT&S Communities across the HCA Enterprise. This person is called on to lead projects and operational initiatives for the department within the realm of automating software delivery solutions for enterprise applications.

Responsibilities:

Enterprise Software Delivery

Develop, test, and deploy enterprise software packages using PowerShell App Deployment Toolkit (PSADT).
Create and maintain SCCM applications and packages for automated software deployment across large-scale environments.
Ensure consistent and reliable delivery of all automated application solutions.
Collaborate with application owners to gather requirements and validate deployment success.
Maintain version control and documentation for all deployment scripts and packages.
Utilize custom test environments to perform proof of concept implementations before moving to pilot/production availability.

PowerShell Scripting & Automation

Design and optimize PowerShell scripts to automate software installations, configurations, and post-deployment tasks.
Troubleshoot and enhance existing PSADT scripts for improved reliability and performance.
Implement logging, error handling, and rollback mechanisms in deployment scripts.

SCCM Infrastructure Support

Assists the team in ongoing support and maintenance of SCCM infrastructure including site servers, distribution points, management points, and SQL databases.
Assists the team in performing routine health checks, capacity planning, and performance tuning of SCCM components.
Assists the team in management of boundary groups, client settings, and site configurations to ensure optimal client communication.
Assists the team in applying SCCM updates and hotfixes, and coordinate with Microsoft support when needed.

General Solution Support

Utilize SCCM dynamic collections, advertisements, queries, and reports to gather necessary endpoint reporting data.
Generate query-based collections and SCCM reports using SQL and WQL.
Contribute to Endpoint Services Dashboard content via Splunk or other data analytics engine to provide business intelligence on the endpoint environment.
Facilitate, Implement & Support changes to the Edge Compatibility Mode sites.xml list.
Create, Maintain and Support the centralized management of enterprise web browsers (e.g., Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge)
Discover, Test and Coordinate the implementation of windows configuration standards via Windows Group Policy settings.

Team Collaboration

Collaborate with key stakeholders (Business and IT&G Leaders, vendors, and other product owners) by leading discussions and actions to drive enterprise software automation delivery, problem resolution, project milestone achievement, and produce business enhancing software delivery solutions.
Coordinates sharing of best practices and policies and procedures.
Participate in activities which improve departmental and organizational performance
Adheres to and supports HCA ITG standards, policies, and procedures.
Maintains and protects confidentiality regarding all aspects of patient care.
Adheres to Code of Conduct and Mission and Value Statement.

Other Qualifications:

5+ years’ scripting and automating software delivery solutions using PowerShell with tools such as PowerShell App Deploy Toolkit.
5+ years’ Systems Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) Administration experience and utilizing centralized systems management systems to administer and deliver software to endpoints in an enterprise sized environment.
3+ years’ experience supporting Windows Operating Systems
Advanced working knowledge of Windows 11
Advanced working knowledge Windows Group Policy and Windows Registry for configuration of platform standards.
Willingness to develop and expand expertise in Microsoft Intune, including endpoint configuration, device compliance policies, and application lifecycle management is required.
Microsoft SCCM or Intune Certification and/or Knowledge Level is preferred.

Physical Demands / Working Conditions:

Prolonged sitting. Local travel in the Metro Nashville area; very limited overnight. On call availability.

jobs.description:
About the position

Pennant Services is seeking an experienced, Intune Engineer to design, implement, and support enterprise endpoint platforms. This role is responsible for the strategy, deployment, and lifecycle management of endpoint solutions including Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Chromebooks, and virtual desktops (Azure Virtual Desktop). This role will partner closely with security, infrastructure, and business stakeholders to deliver reliable, secure, and consistent end-user experiences, while driving continuous improvement in processes that support modern, scalable, and service-oriented IT operations.

Responsibilities

Design, implement, and optimize endpoint solutions across Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Chromebooks, and AVDCollaborate with cross-functional teams (Security, Infrastructure, Application teams) to align solutions and prioritiesDefine and promote technical standards, best practices, and architecture patterns for EUC platformsAct as a senior escalation point for complex endpoint, workspace, and virtual desktop issuesDefine, standardize, and continuously improve EUC operational processes and best practicesEstablish clear documentation for endpoint configurations, policies, troubleshooting, and operational workflowsDrive automation, policy-based management, and self-service capabilities to improve scalability and efficiencyIdentify operational gaps and inefficiencies and lead improvement initiativesEnsure processes support reliability, security, compliance, and a consistent end-user experienceOwn endpoint platform architecture and configuration standards across Windows, MacOS, iOS, Android, Chromebooks, AVDLead implementation and optimization of Microsoft Intune and Azure servicesPartner with Security teams to enforce endpoint security controls, Conditional Access, and Zero Trust principlesEnsure endpoint configurations meet security, compliance, and regulatory requirementsMonitor endpoint health, compliance posture, and platform performanceEnsure endpoint platforms are optimized to support smooth user onboarding and productivity from day oneCollaborate with IT support teams to improve end-user experience and reduce friction during device setup and useSupport consistent configuration and readiness standards for new users, roles, and locationsGather and incorporate end-user feedback to improve usability and service qualityAlign device and workspace solutions to evolving workforce and operational needsRequirements

Proven experience in End User Computing, endpoint engineering, or IT operations.Strong hands-on experience supporting distributed, multi-site, or remote environmentsDeep knowledge of endpoint platforms: Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Chromebooks, and virtual desktop environmentsStrong hands-on experience with Microsoft Intune, Entra ID, and Azure servicesExperience supporting onboarding, access readiness, and user lifecycle processes in regulated environmentsDemonstrated ability to design, implement, and improve technical standards and operational processesStrong communication skills with the ability to collaborate across technical and business teamsNice-to-haves

Experience supporting healthcare organizations or other highly regulated industriesFamiliarity with security, compliance, and data privacy requirements (e.g., HIPAA, Zero Trust principles)Track record of driving automation, standardization, and continuous improvement initiatives within endpoint or workplace teamsBenefits

Full benefits package (medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with match)Paid time off, holiday pay, and professional development

YesJob Description:

GDIT is your place. Make it your own by bringing your ideas and unique perspective to our culture. By owning your opportunity at GDIT, you are helping us ensure today is safe and tomorrow is smarter. Our work depends on a MCM Engineer joining our team to support Government activities in Annapolis Junction, MD or Sterling, VA.

At GDIT, we foster a people-focused and customer-centric environment in delivering Desktop Engineering Services. As an MCM Engineer supporting the Government, you will be a part of a team charged with engineering solutions for software deployment, patch deployment, configuration, IT baselines, importing & testing, etc. within the enterprise.

In this role, a typical day will include:

Utilizing Microsoft MCM to provide user workstation baseline configuration and administration of operating systems

Identifying, analyzing, and resolving system problems with both short-term workarounds and long-term solutions

Writing PowerShell scripts to automate systems administration tasks

Providing guidance and leadership to less-experienced systems engineers

Utilizing MCM to provide security updates, vulnerability patches, hotfixes, and other updates to user workstations and servers

Developing and maintaining baselines images for servers and laptops to add the latest security updates, software versions, hotfixes, patches, etc.

What You’ll Need:

Active TS/SCI clearance with polygraph

Must meet DoD 8570 IAT Level II requirements including one of the following: Security+ CE, CND, SSCP, GSEC, GICSP, CySA+, or CCNA Security.

Bachelor’s Degree in a related technical discipline + 8 years’ experience or the equivalent combination of education, technical certification or training, or work/military experience

Expert level experience with SCCM and WSUS

Solid understanding of VMware vCenter

Functional understanding of Microsoft SQL Server and Active Directory

Expertise in MDT and MCM (capturing images (WIM), creating packages, advertisements, task sequences, operating system deployment (OSD) and queries).

Familiarity with DISA STIGs for Windows operating systems

Expertise with IAVA process including RMF release process, testing, approval, and deployment processes

Solid knowledge of Active Directory Security Groups, OUs, DNS, and GPO management

Desired Certifications

Microsoft Software Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) or equivalent

Microsoft 365 Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator Associate

Microsoft Certified Systems Engineer (MCSE)

GDIT IS YOUR PLACE
At GDIT, the mission is our purpose, and our people are at the center of everything we do.

● Rewards: Comprehensive benefits and wellness packages, 401K with company match, and competitive pay and paid time off
● Community: Award-winning culture of innovation and a military-friendly workplace
OWN YOUR OPPORTUNITY
Explore an enterprise IT career at GDIT and you’ll find endless opportunities to grow alongside colleagues who share your desire to drive operations forward.

jobs.title: Federal Solutions Engineer
jobs.summary: About the Role We are looking for a sharp, trusted, and autonomous Federal Solutions Engineer to support NinjaOne’s expansion into the Public Sector. As an embedded technical partner to our Federal Growth team, you’ll play a critical role in both...
jobs.description:
About the Role

We are looking for a sharp, trusted, and autonomous Federal Solutions Engineer to support NinjaOne’s expansion into the Public Sector. As an embedded technical partner to our Federal Growth team, you’ll play a critical role in both pre-sales strategy and post-sale onboarding, acting as a key translator between technical buyer needs and the broader Product and Engineering teams. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in mission-critical moments, loves navigating complex RFPs and government requirements, and can tailor messaging to both deep technical evaluators and high-level decision makers. You’ll serve as a customer advocate internally while building scalable tools, templates, and processes to support the next generation of federal growth.

Location - This is a Remote role open to candidates within Virginia or Maryland or those willing to relocate unassisted.

Travel Requirement - Up to 40% representing NinjaOne at key industry events, product launches, and customer engagements.

What You’ll Be Doing

Own the technical presales experience for Federal and SLED accounts, guiding customers through evaluation, demo, architecture design, and onboarding phases.

Deliver tailored product demos and technical deep dives that align with customer security standards, compliance needs (FedRAMP, DoD IL2/4/5), and procurement requirements.

Support and guide the implementation of the NinjaOne platform for new public sector customers—ensuring quick time-to-value and customer success.

Act as the technical translator between Sales and Product—surfacing actionable feedback and feature gaps that directly impact deal velocity or retention.

Assist with RFPs, RFIs, and security questionnaires, providing accurate, compliance-aligned technical responses that differentiate NinjaOne’s value.

Troubleshoot and resolve customer technical issues, including endpoint configuration, agent behavior, networking challenges, and integrations.

Collaborate with Engineering and Product Management on solution enhancements that improve the Federal customer experience.

Create and maintain reusable content and lab environments to scale future engagements (demos, battlecards, scripts, templates, etc.).

Represent NinjaOne at industry events, partner summits, and key government-facing conferences as a technical ambassador.

Technical Requirements

Proficiency with Windows environments (registry, services, performance troubleshooting).

Working knowledge of Linux and macOS systems and basic command-line troubleshooting.

Solid foundation in networking and security concepts, including firewalls, VPN, DNS, and common endpoint protections.

Familiarity with Active Directory, remote access tools, and endpoint deployment methodologies.

Hands-on experience with RMM tools, ITSM platforms, and endpoint lifecycle management is highly preferred.

Ability to quickly learn and adapt to new technologies and translate their value to customers.

Experience using Salesforce, Jira, Highspot, or equivalent systems is required.

About You

5+ years in a Sales Engineer, Solutions Architect, or Technical Consultant role—within the Public Sector Enterprise space.

Exceptional communicator—capable of clearly articulating complex information to both technical and non-technical audiences, from system administrators to procurement and leadership.

A calm and credible presence in high-stakes, high-pressure customer conversations.

Customer-first mindset—you genuinely care about their experience and success.

Organized, self-directed, and capable of thriving in a remote-first, fast-paced environment.

Familiarity with FedRAMP, DoD Impact Levels, and NIST frameworks is required.

You align with our values Curiosity, Integrity, Kindness, Humility, Builders and are committed to creating an environment where these values flourish in our teams.

Bachelor’s degree preferred but not required; respected technical certifications (e.g., Security+, MCSE, CISSP) is preferred.

Ability to obtain a security clearance is required.

About Us

NinjaOne automates the hardest parts of IT to deliver visibility, security, and control over all endpoints for more than 20,000 customers. The NinjaOne automated endpoint management platform is proven to increase productivity, reduce security risk, and lower costs for IT teams and managed service providers. NinjaOne is obsessed with customer success and provides free and unlimited onboarding, training, and support. NinjaOne is #1 on G2 in endpoint management, patch management, remote monitoring and management, and mobile device management.

What You’ll Love

We are a collaborative, kind, and curious community.

We honor your flexibility needs with full-time work that is hybrid remote.

We have you covered with our comprehensive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, and vision insurance.

We help you prepare for your financial future with our 401(k) plan.

We prioritize your work-life balance with our unlimited PTO.

We reward your work with opportunity for growth and advancement.

Additional Information

This position is NOT eligible for Visa sponsorship.

Starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to location, market demands, experience, job-related knowledge, and skills. The benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage and PTO. For roles based in Maryland, the base salary hiring range for this position is $140,000 to $140,000 per year with On Target Earnings of $200,000 to $200,000 per year.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by applicable law. We are committed to providing an inclusive and diverse work environment.
